<project title="inguitive" summary="A pure Python web framework for building reactive, interactive web applications without writing JavaScript. Combines FastAPI, HTMX, and Tailwind CSS with a declarative component model and session-scoped reactive state management.">inguitive lets Python developers define pages and trigger handlers entirely in Python. Components automatically re-render when state changes via HTMX out-of-band swaps. Each browser session has isolated state; no global mutable variables are needed. The public API is exported from src/inguitive/__init__.py.<docs><doc title="README" desc="Full quick-start guide, component reference, session backend comparison, and production deployment checklist"># inguitive

A pure Python web framework combining intuitive syntax with **HTMX** for partial page reloads and **Tailwind CSS** for styling.

Unlike traditional request-response frameworks, inguitive provides reactive state management where components automatically re-render when state changes, eliminating the need for manual DOM manipulation or JavaScript. It is designed for Python developers who want to build interactive web applications using only Python, without sacrificing the dynamic feel of modern SPAs.

## Features

- **Reactive State Management**: Components automatically re-render when state changes
- **HTMX Integration**: Out-of-band swaps for seamless partial page updates
- **Component-Based**: Composable UI components with clean Python syntax
- **Dynamic Attributes**: All component attributes can be static strings or callables
- **Trigger Arguments**: Pass data from components to handlers via `trigger_args` and `get_trigger_args()`
- **Type Safe**: Full type hints throughout the codebase
- **Tailwind CSS**: First-class support for utility-first styling

## Quick Start

### Installation

```bash
pip install inguitive
```

### Basic Example

```python
from inguitive import Div, Button, Label, State, create_app
from inguitive.css import BUTTON_PRIMARY_CSS

# Create FastAPI app
app = create_app()

# Create reactive state
counter_state = State(0, &quot;counter_state&quot;)

# Define a trigger function
@app.trigger_handler
def increment():
    counter_state.set(counter_state.get() + 1)

# Define a component
def Counter():
    return Div(
        Label(text=lambda: f&quot;Count: {counter_state.get()}&quot;, id=&quot;counter-label&quot;, listen_to=&quot;counter_state&quot;),
        Button(&quot;+1&quot;, trigger=&quot;increment&quot;, css=BUTTON_PRIMARY_CSS),
    )

# Define a route function
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def index():
    return Counter()
```

### Trigger Arguments

Pass data from a component to its handler using `trigger_args` on the component and `get_trigger_args()` in the handler:

```python
from inguitive import Button, Div, State, Text, create_app, get_trigger_args

app = create_app()
selected_state = State(&quot;none&quot;, &quot;selected_state&quot;)

@app.trigger_handler
def select_item():
    item_id = get_trigger_args().get(&quot;id&quot;)
    selected_state.set(item_id)

@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def index():
    return Div(
        Button(&quot;Select A&quot;, trigger=&quot;select_item&quot;, trigger_args={&quot;id&quot;: &quot;a&quot;}),
        Button(&quot;Select B&quot;, trigger=&quot;select_item&quot;, trigger_args={&quot;id&quot;: &quot;b&quot;}),
        Text(lambda: f&quot;Selected: {selected_state.get()}&quot;, listen_to=&quot;selected_state&quot;),
    )
```

`trigger_args` are passed as URL query parameters; `get_trigger_args()` returns them as a `dict[str, str]` inside the handler.

## Component Reference

inguitive provides a comprehensive set of components organized by category. All components support dynamic attributes via callables and can listen to state changes for automatic re-rendering.

### Base Components

| Component | Description | Key Parameters | Example |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| **`Component`** | Base class for all components | `id`, `css`, `listen_to` | Custom component base |
| **`TemplateComponent`** | Render Jinja2 templates | `template`, context vars | Custom HTML with templating |

### Layout Components

| Component | Description | Key Parameters | Example |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| **`Div`** | Container div element | `*children`, `id`, `css` | `Div(Button(&quot;Click&quot;), css=&quot;flex gap-2&quot;)` |
| **`Text`** | Paragraph/text element | `text`, `id`, `css` | `Text(&quot;Hello&quot;, css=&quot;text-xl&quot;)` |
| **`Label`** | Form label element | `text`, `for_`, `id`, `css` | `Label(&quot;Name:&quot;, for_=&quot;name&quot;)` |

### Form Components

| Component | Description | Key Parameters | Example |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| **`Form`** | Form container | `*children`, `action`, `method` | `Form(Input(...), Button(...))` |
| **`Input`** | Text input field | `type`, `value`, `placeholder`, `listen_to` | `Input(id=&quot;email&quot;, type=&quot;email&quot;)` |
| **`Textarea`** | Multi-line text input | `value`, `placeholder`, `rows` | `Textarea(id=&quot;bio&quot;, rows=5)` |
| **`Select`** | Dropdown select | `options`, `value`, `listen_to` | `Select(id=&quot;country&quot;, options=[...])` |
| **`Checkbox`** | Checkbox input | `checked`, `id`, `listen_to` | `Checkbox(id=&quot;agree&quot;, checked=True)` |
| **`Radio`** | Radio button input | `value`, `checked`, `name` | `Radio(id=&quot;male&quot;, name=&quot;gender&quot;)` |
| **`Button`** | Clickable button | `*children`, `trigger`, `css` | `Button(&quot;Click&quot;, trigger=&quot;action&quot;)` |

### Navigation Components

| Component | Description | Key Parameters | Example |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| **`Link`** | Semantic navigation link | `*children`, `href`, `css` | `Link(&quot;Home&quot;, href=&quot;/&quot;)` |

### Data Display Components

| Component | Description | Key Parameters | Example |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| **`DataTable`** | Tabular data display | `data`, `columns`, `css` | `DataTable(data=[{&quot;name&quot;: &quot;A&quot;}])` |
| **`Icon`** | SVG icon component | `svg`, `css` | `Icon(&quot;&lt;svg ...&gt;...&lt;/svg&gt;&quot;, css=&quot;w-6 h-6&quot;)` |

### Common Parameters (All Components)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `str \| None` | HTML id attribute. Required for state listening and OOB updates |
| `css` | `str \| Callable[[], str] \| dict \| None` | Tailwind CSS classes. For DataTable, can be a dict with keys: `table`, `header`, `row`, `cell` |
| `listen_to` | `str \| list[str] \| None` | State name(s) to listen for changes. Triggers re-render when state updates |
| `trigger` | `str \| None` | Trigger name for HTMX POST actions (Button, Input, etc.) |
| `trigger_args` | `dict[str, str] \| None` | Query parameters to pass with trigger |

## Navigation &amp; Actions

Use `Link` for traditional navigation (SEO, bookmarking, new-tab support) and `trigger` for partial page updates:

| | `Link(href=&quot;...&quot;)` | `trigger=&quot;...&quot;` |
|---|---|---|
| Renders | `&lt;a href=&quot;...&quot;&gt;` | HTMX POST |
| URL changes | ✅ | ❌ |
| Open in new tab | ✅ | ❌ |

```python
from inguitive import Link, Button

# Traditional navigation
Link(&quot;Home&quot;, href=&quot;/&quot;)
Link(&quot;Documentation&quot;, href=&quot;/docs&quot;, css=&quot;text-blue-500&quot;)

# Partial updates
Button(&quot;Save&quot;, trigger=&quot;save_form&quot;)
Button(&quot;Like&quot;, trigger=&quot;like_post&quot;, trigger_args={&quot;id&quot;: &quot;123&quot;})
```

## Project Structure

```
.
├── src/
│   └── inguitive/
│       ├── __init__.py      # Public API
│       ├── components.py    # Component classes
│       ├── state.py         # Reactive state
│       ├── htmx.py           # HTMX helpers
│       ├── fastapi.py       # FastAPI integration
│       └── svg.py           # SVG icon definitions
├── examples/
│   ├── counter_app.py        # Per-session counter with theme toggle
│   ├── todo_app.py           # CRUD with filtering and real-time count
│   ├── chat_app.py           # Real-time chat
│   ├── navigation_demo.py    # Link vs trigger patterns
│   ├── registration_form.py  # Form handling
│   └── data_table_app.py     # DataTable with sorting and filtering
├── tests/
│   └── test_*.py           # Test files
├── pyproject.toml           # Build configuration
└── README.md
```

## Session Backends

inguitive uses session-scoped registries to isolate user state. Choose a backend based on your deployment needs:

| Backend | Use When | Persistence | Multi-Worker |
|---------|----------|-------------|--------------|
| **`MemoryBackend`** | Development, single worker | ❌ No (lost on restart) | ❌ No |
| **`RedisBackend`** | Production, multiple workers | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |

**MemoryBackend** (default) stores sessions in RAM - perfect for development. **RedisBackend** stores sessions in Redis for production deployments with multiple workers or persistent sessions.

```python
from inguitive import create_app, MemoryBackend, RedisBackend

# Development: In-memory sessions (default, no config needed)
app = create_app()

# Or explicitly:
app = create_app(session_backend=MemoryBackend())

# Production: Redis-backed sessions for scaling
app = create_app(
    session_backend=RedisBackend(
        redis_url=&quot;redis://localhost:6379&quot;,
        ttl_seconds=3600  # Session timeout: 1 hour
    )
)
```

Requires `pip install redis` for RedisBackend.

### Session Lifetime and Expiry

inguitive sessions are created automatically on first request and persist across page reloads. Each session has isolated component, state, and data registries.

**Session Creation:** A new session is created with a unique ID when a user first visits your application. The session ID is stored in a cookie.

**Session Persistence:** Sessions persist across page reloads and browser navigation within the same domain. The session cookie maintains the session ID, allowing the framework to restore the user&#x27;s state.

**Session Expiry:**
- **MemoryBackend:** Sessions expire after `ttl_seconds` (default: 3600 = 1 hour) of inactivity. Expired sessions are automatically cleaned up every N requests (configurable via `session_cleanup_interval`).
- **RedisBackend:** Sessions are stored in Redis with a TTL. Redis automatically expires keys after the configured `ttl_seconds`, providing automatic cleanup.

**Differences Between Backends:**

| Aspect | MemoryBackend | RedisBackend |
|--------|---------------|--------------|
| Persistence | Lost on process restart | Survives process restarts |
| Multi-worker | Not supported (shared memory) | Supported (Redis as shared store) |
| Cleanup | Manual/Periodic via `cleanup_expired()` | Automatic via Redis TTL |
| Use Case | Development, testing | Production, scaling |

**Page Reload Behavior:** Session state is preserved across page reloads. Components listening to state will re-render with the current state values when the page loads.

## Production Deployment

Before deploying your inguitive app to production, configure these security settings:

```python
from inguitive import create_app, RedisBackend

app = create_app(
    session_backend=RedisBackend(redis_url=&quot;redis://localhost:6379&quot;),
    session_cookie_secure=True,      # Cookies only over HTTPS
    session_cookie_httponly=True,    # Prevent JavaScript access (default)
    session_cookie_max_age=86400,    # 24-hour session timeout
)
```

**Checklist:**
- ✅ Use `RedisBackend` (not `MemoryBackend`) for persistence across workers
- ✅ Set `session_cookie_secure=True` when using HTTPS
- ✅ Verify `session_cookie_httponly=True` (enabled by default)
- ✅ Deploy with HTTPS (required for secure cookies)

## Running the Demo

```bash
# From the repository root
uvicorn examples.counter_app:app --reload

# Then open http://localhost:8000
```

</doc><doc title="CHANGELOG" desc="Version history and versioning policy (SemVer)"># inguitive Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [0.1.0] - 2026-07-17

### Added

#### Core Features

**Reactive State Management**
- `State` class for managing application state
- Automatic component re-rendering when state changes
- Listener tracking via `listen_to` parameter
- Auto-propagation of state updates to listening components
- Context-local mutation tracking

**HTMX Integration**
- Native HTMX attribute support on all components
- Out-of-band (OOB) swap functionality via `update_components()`
- Trigger handlers with `@app.trigger_handler` decorator
- Form data injection in trigger handlers
- Async trigger handler support
- Trigger argument context via `get_trigger_args()`

**Component System**
- 15 built-in components: `Component`, `Div`, `Button`, `Label`, `Icon`, `Input`, `Textarea`, `Select`, `Checkbox`, `Radio`, `Form`, `Text`, `Link`, `TemplateComponent`, `DataTable`
- All component attributes support dynamic values via callables
- Composable UI components with clean Python syntax
- Tailwind CSS first-class support for all components
- `DataTable` supports dict-based CSS for per-element styling (`table`, `header`, `row`, `cell` keys)

**Session Management**
- Per-session state isolation (each browser has independent state)
- `MemoryBackend` for development (in-memory storage)
- `RedisBackend` for production (Redis-based persistence)
- Session expiry and automatic memory cleanup
- Session ID management utilities

**FastAPI Integration**
- `InguitiveApp` Protocol for type-safe access to `@app.page` and `@app.trigger_handler` decorators
- `create_app()` factory for easy app creation
- `@app.page` decorator for page routes
- `@app.trigger_handler` decorator for trigger handlers
- `redirect()` and `run_app()` utilities

**Styling**
- First-class Tailwind CSS support
- Predefined CSS constants (`BUTTON_BASE_CSS`, `BUTTON_PRIMARY_CSS`, `BUTTON_SECONDARY_CSS`)
- Dynamic CSS via callables for all components
- Per-component CSS customization

#### Example Applications
- `counter_app.py`: Per-session isolation with counter and theme toggle
- `todo_app.py`: CRUD operations with filtering and real-time count
- `chat_app.py`: Real-time chat demonstration
- `navigation_demo.py`: Navigation patterns (Link vs trigger)
- `registration_form.py`: Form handling demonstration
- `data_table_app.py`: Data table with sorting and filtering

#### Test Coverage
- 11 test files covering all major functionality
- Tests for async handlers, components, decorators
- Tests for form data injection, session backends, state isolation
- Tests for trigger arguments

### Fixed
- Fixed `dynamic()` evaluating at call time instead of render time
- Fixed `RedisBackend` serialization of component registry
- Fixed `MemoryBackend` class-level variable shared across instances

---

## Versioning Policy

This project follows [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html):

- **MAJOR** version bumps for backward-incompatible API changes
- **MINOR** version bumps for backward-compatible new functionality
- **PATCH** version bumps for backward-compatible bug fixes
</doc><doc title="Public API" desc="All exported names — components, State, create_app, session backends, helpers">&quot;&quot;&quot;
inguitive - A pure Python web framework combining intuitive syntax with HTMX and Tailwind CSS.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from inguitive.components import (
    Button,
    Checkbox,
    Component,
    DataTable,
    Div,
    Form,
    Icon,
    Input,
    Label,
    Link,
    Radio,
    Select,
    TemplateComponent,
    Text,
    Textarea,
)

# Re-export styling constants for convenience
from inguitive.css import (
    BUTTON_BASE_CSS,
    BUTTON_PRIMARY_CSS,
    BUTTON_SECONDARY_CSS,
)
from inguitive.fastapi import InguitiveApp, create_app, redirect, run_app
from inguitive.htmx import update_components
from inguitive.session import (
    MemoryBackend,
    RedisBackend,
    Session,
    SessionBackend,
    get_session_backend,
    get_session_id,
    set_session_backend,
)
from inguitive.state import State

# Re-export SVG icons for convenience
from inguitive.svg import MOON, SUN
from inguitive.trigger import get_trigger_args
from inguitive.utils import nl2br

__all__ = [
    # Components
    &quot;Component&quot;,
    &quot;Div&quot;,
    &quot;Button&quot;,
    &quot;Label&quot;,
    &quot;Icon&quot;,
    &quot;Input&quot;,
    &quot;Textarea&quot;,
    &quot;Select&quot;,
    &quot;Checkbox&quot;,
    &quot;Radio&quot;,
    &quot;Form&quot;,
    &quot;Text&quot;,
    &quot;Link&quot;,
    &quot;TemplateComponent&quot;,
    &quot;DataTable&quot;,
    # State
    &quot;State&quot;,
    # HTMX helpers
    &quot;update_components&quot;,
    # Trigger
    &quot;get_trigger_args&quot;,
    # Helpers
    # FastAPI
    &quot;InguitiveApp&quot;,
    &quot;create_app&quot;,
    &quot;redirect&quot;,
    &quot;run_app&quot;,
    # Session
    &quot;Session&quot;,
    &quot;SessionBackend&quot;,
    &quot;MemoryBackend&quot;,
    &quot;RedisBackend&quot;,
    &quot;set_session_backend&quot;,
    &quot;get_session_backend&quot;,
    &quot;get_session_id&quot;,
    # Styling
    &quot;BUTTON_BASE_CSS&quot;,
    &quot;BUTTON_PRIMARY_CSS&quot;,
    &quot;BUTTON_SECONDARY_CSS&quot;,
    # Icons
    &quot;MOON&quot;,
    &quot;SUN&quot;,
    # Utilities
    &quot;nl2br&quot;,
]

__version__ = "0.1.1"
</doc><doc title="Components" desc="All UI component classes (Div, Button, Input, DataTable, etc.) with render/update methods">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Component classes for inguitive framework.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from collections.abc import Callable, Mapping

import jinja2

from inguitive.session import _get_component_registry


class Component:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Base component class for inguitive.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        trigger: str | None = None,
        trigger_args: dict[str, str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        # Generate UUID if no id provided
        if id is None:
            id = f&quot;comp-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}&quot;
        self.id = id
        self.css = css

        # Handle action parameters (trigger = POST)
        if trigger:
            url = f&quot;/_trigger/{trigger.lstrip(&#x27;/&#x27;)}&quot;
            if trigger_args:
                url += &quot;?&quot; + &quot;&amp;&quot;.join(f&quot;{k}={v}&quot; for k, v in trigger_args.items())
            attrs.setdefault(&quot;hx-post&quot;, url)
            attrs.setdefault(&quot;hx-target&quot;, &quot;#hx-target&quot;)

        self.attrs = attrs
        _get_component_registry()[self.id] = self
        if listen_to:
            from inguitive.state import _get_state_by_name

            # Normalize to list for uniform handling
            state_names = [listen_to] if isinstance(listen_to, str) else listen_to
            for state_name in state_names:
                state = _get_state_by_name(state_name)
                if state is not None:
                    state.add_listener(self.id)

    def _resolve(self, value: str | Callable[[], str]) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Resolve a potentially dynamic value (callable or static).&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return value() if callable(value) else value

    def _get_attrs_str(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Convert attributes to HTML string, handling css -&gt; class conversion and dynamic values.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        filtered_attrs = {}
        for k, v in self.attrs.items():
            if k != &quot;css&quot;:
                filtered_attrs[k] = self._resolve(v)
        resolved_css = self._resolve(self.css) if self.css else None
        if resolved_css:
            filtered_attrs[&quot;class&quot;] = resolved_css
        # Add id if present
        if self.id:
            filtered_attrs[&quot;id&quot;] = self.id
        return &quot; &quot;.join(f&#x27;{k}=&quot;{v}&quot;&#x27; for k, v in filtered_attrs.items())

    def _render_children(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render all children of this component.

        Handles both direct child components and callable/lambda children.
        Supports nested lists of children.
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        children_html_parts = []
        for child in getattr(self, &quot;children&quot;, []):
            if isinstance(child, Component):
                children_html_parts.append(child.render())
            else:
                resolved = self._resolve(child)
                if isinstance(resolved, list):
                    for item in resolved:
                        if isinstance(item, Component):
                            children_html_parts.append(item.render())
                        else:
                            children_html_parts.append(str(item))
                else:
                    if isinstance(resolved, Component):
                        children_html_parts.append(resolved.render())
                    else:
                        children_html_parts.append(str(resolved))
        return &quot;&quot;.join(children_html_parts)

    def _oob_attrs_str(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Get attributes string with hx-swap-oob for out-of-band updates.

        Returns regular attributes if self.id is not set.
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self._get_attrs_str()
        return f&#x27;hx-swap-oob=&quot;true&quot; {self._get_attrs_str()}&#x27;.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _normalize_children(children: tuple) -&gt; list:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Normalize children tuple to a list.

        Supports both: Component(a, b) and Component([a, b]).

        Args:
            children: Tuple of child components/strings from *children

        Returns:
            List of children (flattened if single element was a list)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if len(children) == 1 and isinstance(children[0], list):
            return list(children[0])
        return list(children)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        raise NotImplementedError


class Div(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML div component.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, *children, id: str | None = None, css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None, **attrs):
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, **attrs)
        self.children = self._normalize_children(children)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;div {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/div&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;div {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/div&gt;&quot;


class Button(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML button component with HTMX support.

    Use trigger, navigate, or redirect parameters for click actions:
    - trigger: POST action for partial updates (replaces old on_click)
    - navigate: GET navigation for full page changes
    - redirect: Immediate browser redirect
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, *children, id: str | None = None, css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None, **attrs):
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, **attrs)
        self.children = self._normalize_children(children)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;button {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/button&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;button {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/button&gt;&quot;


class Label(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML label component.

    Renders a &lt;label&gt; element. Use for_ parameter to associate with input elements.

    Example:
        Label(&quot;Username&quot;, for_=&quot;username-input&quot;)
        Label(&quot;Remember me&quot;, for_=&quot;remember&quot;, css=&quot;text-sm&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        text: str | Callable[[], str],
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        for_: str | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Label component.

        Args:
            text: Label text content
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            for_: ID of the form element this label is for (uses &#x27;for&#x27; HTML attribute)
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if for_ is not None:
            attrs[&quot;for&quot;] = for_
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, **attrs)
        self.text = text

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        resolved_text = self._resolve(self.text)
        return f&quot;&lt;label {attrs}&gt;{resolved_text}&lt;/label&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        resolved_text = self._resolve(self.text)
        return f&quot;&lt;label {attrs}&gt;{resolved_text}&lt;/label&gt;&quot;


class Link(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML link/anchor component for semantic navigation.

    Renders a standard &lt;a&gt; tag. Use for traditional links where semantic
    HTML matters (SEO, accessibility, browser behavior).

    Supports children like Div and Button, allowing nested components.

    Example:
        Link(&quot;Home&quot;, href=&quot;/&quot;)
        Link(Text(&quot;Documentation&quot;), href=&quot;/docs&quot;, css=&quot;text-blue-500 hover:underline&quot;)
        Link(Icon(HOME_SVG), href=&quot;/&quot;, css=&quot;w-6 h-6&quot;)
        Link(Button(&quot;Click&quot;), href=&quot;/page1&quot;)
        Link([Text(&quot;A&quot;), Text(&quot;B&quot;)], href=&quot;/&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        *children,
        href: str,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Link component.

        Args:
            *children: Link content (strings, Components, or callables)
            href: URL to link to
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (target, rel, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, **attrs)
        if href:
            self.attrs[&quot;href&quot;] = href
        self.children = self._normalize_children(children)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;a {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/a&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;a {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/a&gt;&quot;


class Text(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML paragraph/text component.

    Renders a &lt;p&gt; tag for paragraph text content. Use for standalone text blocks,
    descriptions, and any content that isn&#x27;t a form label.

    Example:
        Text(&quot;Welcome to our application&quot;)
        Text(&quot;This is a paragraph&quot;, css=&quot;text-gray-600 mt-4&quot;)
        Text(lambda: get_description(), listen_to=&quot;desc_state&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self, text: str | Callable[[], str], id: str | None = None, css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None, **attrs
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Text component.

        Args:
            text: Text content (string or callable returning string)
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, **attrs)
        self.text = text

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        resolved_text = self._resolve(self.text)
        return f&quot;&lt;p {attrs}&gt;{resolved_text}&lt;/p&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        resolved_text = self._resolve(self.text)
        return f&quot;&lt;p {attrs}&gt;{resolved_text}&lt;/p&gt;&quot;


class Icon(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;SVG icon component.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, svg: str | Callable[[], str], css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None, **attrs):
        super().__init__(css=css, **attrs)
        self.svg = svg

    @staticmethod
    def _replace_class(svg_str: str, css_value: str) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Replace or insert class attribute in SVG string.

        Preserves all other attributes, quote style, and structure.

        Args:
            svg_str: The SVG HTML string
            css_value: The new class value (without quotes)

        Returns:
            SVG string with updated class attribute
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Find class attribute with optional whitespace between class and =
        idx = svg_str.find(&quot;class&quot;)
        if idx != -1:
            # Move past &#x27;class&#x27;
            idx += len(&quot;class&quot;)
            # Skip whitespace
            while idx &lt; len(svg_str) and svg_str[idx] in &quot; \t&quot;:
                idx += 1
            # Check for &#x27;=&#x27;
            if idx &lt; len(svg_str) and svg_str[idx] == &quot;=&quot;:
                idx += 1
                # Skip whitespace after &#x27;=&#x27;
                while idx &lt; len(svg_str) and svg_str[idx] in &quot; \t&quot;:
                    idx += 1
                # Now at the opening quote
                if idx &lt; len(svg_str) and svg_str[idx] in (&#x27;&quot;&#x27;, &quot;&#x27;&quot;):
                    quote_char = svg_str[idx]
                    # Find closing quote
                    content_start = idx + 1
                    content_end = svg_str.find(quote_char, content_start)
                    if content_end != -1:
                        # Replace the content between quotes
                        return svg_str[:content_start] + css_value + svg_str[content_end:]
        # No class attribute found - insert one
        if svg_str.startswith(&quot;&lt;svg&quot;):
            pos = len(&quot;&lt;svg&quot;)
            return svg_str[:pos] + f&#x27; class=&quot;{css_value}&quot;&#x27; + svg_str[pos:]
        return f&#x27;&lt;svg class=&quot;{css_value}&quot;&gt;{svg_str}&#x27;

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        resolved_svg: str = self._resolve(self.svg)

        if self.css:
            resolved_css: str = self._resolve(self.css)
            resolved_svg = self._replace_class(resolved_svg, resolved_css)

        return resolved_svg


class Input(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML input component for text, email, password, etc.

    Example:
        Input(id=&quot;email&quot;, type=&quot;email&quot;, placeholder=&quot;Enter email&quot;, css=&quot;border rounded p-2&quot;)
        Input(id=&quot;name&quot;, value=state, listen_to=&quot;name_state&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        type: str = &quot;text&quot;,
        value: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        placeholder: str = &quot;&quot;,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize an Input component.

        Args:
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            type: Input type (text, email, password, number, etc.)
            value: Initial value (string or callable)
            placeholder: Placeholder text
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (name, required, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Set default value
        if value is not None:
            attrs[&quot;value&quot;] = value
        if placeholder:
            attrs[&quot;placeholder&quot;] = placeholder
        if type != &quot;text&quot;:
            attrs[&quot;type&quot;] = type
        # Auto-set name to id if not provided
        if &quot;name&quot; not in attrs and id is not None:
            attrs[&quot;name&quot;] = id
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the input element.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;


class Textarea(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML textarea component for multi-line text input.

    Example:
        Textarea(id=&quot;bio&quot;, placeholder=&quot;Tell us about yourself&quot;, rows=5)
        Textarea(id=&quot;notes&quot;, value=content_state, listen_to=&quot;notes_state&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        value: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        placeholder: str = &quot;&quot;,
        rows: int = 3,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Textarea component.

        Args:
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            value: Initial value (string or callable)
            placeholder: Placeholder text
            rows: Number of visible rows
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (name, required, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if placeholder:
            attrs[&quot;placeholder&quot;] = placeholder
        if rows:
            attrs[&quot;rows&quot;] = str(rows)
        # Auto-set name to id if not provided
        if &quot;name&quot; not in attrs and id is not None:
            attrs[&quot;name&quot;] = id
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)
        self.value = value

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the textarea element.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        # Textarea content goes between tags, not in value attribute
        resolved_value = self._resolve(self.value) if self.value else &quot;&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;textarea {attrs}&gt;{resolved_value}&lt;/textarea&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        resolved_value = self._resolve(self.value) if self.value else &quot;&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;textarea {attrs}&gt;{resolved_value}&lt;/textarea&gt;&quot;


class Select(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML select dropdown component.

    Example:
        Select(id=&quot;country&quot;, options=[(&quot;us&quot;, &quot;USA&quot;), (&quot;de&quot;, &quot;Germany&quot;)], value=&quot;us&quot;)
        Select(id=&quot;theme&quot;, options=[(&quot;light&quot;, &quot;Light&quot;), (&quot;dark&quot;, &quot;Dark&quot;)],
               value=lambda: theme_state.get(), listen_to=&quot;theme_state&quot;)
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        options: list[tuple[str, str]] | Callable[[], list[tuple[str, str]]] | None = None,
        value: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Select component.

        Args:
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            options: List of (value, display_text) tuples, or callable returning such list
            value: Selected value (string or callable)
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (name, required, disabled, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Auto-set name to id if not provided
        if &quot;name&quot; not in attrs and id is not None:
            attrs[&quot;name&quot;] = id
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)
        self.options = options or []
        self.value = value

    def _render_options(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render all option elements.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        resolved_options = self._resolve(self.options) if self.options else []  # type: ignore
        resolved_value = self._resolve(self.value) if self.value else None
        option_tags = []
        for val, text in resolved_options:  # type: ignore[str-unpack]
            selected = &quot; selected&quot; if val == resolved_value else &quot;&quot;
            option_tags.append(f&#x27;&lt;option value=&quot;{val}&quot;{selected}&gt;{text}&lt;/option&gt;&#x27;)
        return &quot;&quot;.join(option_tags)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the select element with options.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        options_html = self._render_options()
        return f&quot;&lt;select {attrs}&gt;{options_html}&lt;/select&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        options_html = self._render_options()
        return f&quot;&lt;select {attrs}&gt;{options_html}&lt;/select&gt;&quot;


class Checkbox(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML checkbox input component.

    Renders only the &lt;input type=&quot;checkbox&quot;&gt; element. Use with Label and Div
    for composed structures.

    Example:
        Div(
            Checkbox(id=&quot;agree&quot;, checked=True),
            Label(&quot;I agree to terms&quot;, for_=&quot;agree&quot;),
            css=&quot;flex items-center gap-2&quot;
        )
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        checked: bool | Callable[[], bool] = False,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Checkbox component.

        Args:
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            checked: Checked state (boolean or callable)
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (name, required, disabled, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Set type to checkbox
        attrs[&quot;type&quot;] = &quot;checkbox&quot;
        # Auto-set name to id if not provided
        if &quot;name&quot; not in attrs and id is not None:
            attrs[&quot;name&quot;] = id
        # Store checked state
        self.checked = checked
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the checkbox input element.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        resolved_checked = self._resolve(self.checked) if self.checked else False  # type: ignore
        if resolved_checked:
            attrs += &quot; checked&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        resolved_checked = self._resolve(self.checked) if self.checked else False  # type: ignore
        if resolved_checked:
            attrs += &quot; checked&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;


class Radio(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML radio input component.

    Renders only the &lt;input type=&quot;radio&quot;&gt; element. Use with Label and Div
    for composed radio groups.

    Example:
        Div(
            Radio(id=&quot;male&quot;, name=&quot;gender&quot;, value=&quot;male&quot;, checked=True),
            Label(&quot;Male&quot;, for_=&quot;male&quot;),
            Radio(id=&quot;female&quot;, name=&quot;gender&quot;, value=&quot;female&quot;),
            Label(&quot;Female&quot;, for_=&quot;female&quot;),
            css=&quot;flex gap-4&quot;
        )
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        value: str = &quot;&quot;,
        checked: bool | Callable[[], bool] = False,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Radio component.

        Args:
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            value: Value for this radio option
            checked: Checked state (boolean or callable)
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (name, required, disabled, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Set type to radio
        attrs[&quot;type&quot;] = &quot;radio&quot;
        if value:
            attrs[&quot;value&quot;] = value
        # Auto-set name to id if not provided
        if &quot;name&quot; not in attrs and id is not None:
            attrs[&quot;name&quot;] = id
        # Store checked state
        self.checked = checked
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the radio input element.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        resolved_checked = self._resolve(self.checked) if self.checked else False  # type: ignore
        if resolved_checked:
            attrs += &quot; checked&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        resolved_checked = self._resolve(self.checked) if self.checked else False  # type: ignore
        if resolved_checked:
            attrs += &quot; checked&quot;
        return f&quot;&lt;input {attrs}&gt;&quot;


class Form(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML form component for grouping input elements.

    Example:
        Form(
            Input(id=&quot;name&quot;, name=&quot;name&quot;),
            Select(id=&quot;country&quot;, name=&quot;country&quot;, options=[...]),
            Button(&quot;Submit&quot;, type=&quot;submit&quot;),
            action=&quot;/submit&quot;,
            method=&quot;POST&quot;
        )
        Form(Button(&quot;Save&quot;, trigger=&quot;save&quot;), ...)  # HTMX form with trigger

    Note: Forms with triggers automatically reset after successful submission.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        *children,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        action: str = &quot;&quot;,
        method: str = &quot;post&quot;,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a Form component.

        Args:
            *children: Form elements (Input, Textarea, Select, Button, etc.)
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            action: Form action URL
            method: HTTP method (get, post, etc.)
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (hx-post, hx-target, etc.)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if action:
            attrs[&quot;action&quot;] = action
        if method:
            attrs[&quot;method&quot;] = method
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)
        self.children = self._normalize_children(children)

        # Auto-add form reset handler for HTMX forms
        # This clears the form after successful submission
        if self.attrs.get(&quot;hx-post&quot;) and &quot;hx-on::after-request&quot; not in self.attrs:
            self.attrs[&quot;hx-on::after-request&quot;] = &quot;if(event.detail.successful) this.reset()&quot;

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the form with children.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        attrs = self._get_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;form {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/form&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        children_html = self._render_children()
        return f&quot;&lt;form {attrs}&gt;{children_html}&lt;/form&gt;&quot;


class TemplateComponent(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Component that renders a Jinja2 template.

    Allows embedding complex HTML structures with dynamic content using Jinja2 templating.

    Example:
        # Inline template
        TemplateComponent(
            template=&#x27;&lt;div class=&quot;{{ css }}&quot;&gt;{{ content }}&lt;/div&gt;&#x27;,
            content=&quot;Hello World&quot;,
            css=&quot;text-red-500&quot;
        )

        # Template with state
        TemplateComponent(
            template=&#x27;&lt;span&gt;{{ value }}&lt;/span&gt;&#x27;,
            value=my_state.get,
            listen_to=&quot;my_state&quot;
        )
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        template: str,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **context,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a TemplateComponent.

        Args:
            template: Jinja2 template string with placeholders
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: Tailwind CSS classes
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **context: Variables to pass to the template
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        super().__init__(id=id, css=css, listen_to=listen_to)
        self.template_str = template
        self.context = context

    @classmethod
    def from_file(
        cls,
        template_path: str,
        id: str | None = None,
        css_name: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **context,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Create a TemplateComponent from a template file.

        Args:
            template_path: Path to the Jinja2 template file
            id: HTML id attribute
            css_name: Tailwind CSS classes
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes
            **context: Variables to pass to the template
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        with open(template_path) as f:
            template_str = f.read()
        return cls(template_str, id=id, css=css_name, listen_to=listen_to, **context)

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the template with context variables.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Resolve all context values
        resolved_context = {}
        for key, value in self.context.items():
            resolved_context[key] = self._resolve(value) if callable(value) else value  # type: ignore

        # Add component attributes to context
        resolved_context[&quot;id&quot;] = self.id
        if self.css:
            resolved_context[&quot;css&quot;] = self._resolve(self.css)

        # Create Jinja2 environment and render
        env = jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.BaseLoader(), autoescape=jinja2.select_autoescape([&quot;html&quot;, &quot;xml&quot;]))
        template = env.from_string(self.template_str)
        return template.render(**resolved_context)

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()
        attrs = self._oob_attrs_str()
        # Render template content
        env = jinja2.Environment(loader=jinja2.BaseLoader(), autoescape=jinja2.select_autoescape([&quot;html&quot;, &quot;xml&quot;]))
        template = env.from_string(self.template_str)
        resolved_context = {}
        for key, value in self.context.items():
            resolved_context[key] = self._resolve(value) if callable(value) else value  # type: ignore
        resolved_context[&quot;id&quot;] = self.id
        if self.css:
            resolved_context[&quot;css&quot;] = self._resolve(self.css)
        content = template.render(**resolved_context)
        return f&quot;&lt;div {attrs}&gt;{content}&lt;/div&gt;&quot;


class DataTable(Component):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;HTML table component for rendering tabular data.

    Renders a list of dictionaries as an HTML table. Each dictionary represents
    a row, and keys represent column names. Supports optional column ordering
    and fine-grained CSS styling.

    Example:
        # Basic usage with automatic column detection
        DataTable(
            data=[
                {&quot;name&quot;: &quot;Alice&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: 30},
                {&quot;name&quot;: &quot;Bob&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: 25}
            ],
            css=&quot;w-full&quot;
        )

        # With explicit column order
        DataTable(
            data=[
                {&quot;name&quot;: &quot;Alice&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: 30, &quot;city&quot;: &quot;NYC&quot;},
                {&quot;name&quot;: &quot;Bob&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: 25, &quot;city&quot;: &quot;LA&quot;}
            ],
            columns=[&quot;name&quot;, &quot;city&quot;, &quot;age&quot;],
            css=&quot;w-full&quot;
        )

        # With dynamic data from state
        DataTable(
            data=table_data_state.get,
            listen_to=&quot;table_data_state&quot;,
            columns=[&quot;id&quot;, &quot;name&quot;, &quot;status&quot;]
        )

        # With dictionary-based CSS for fine-grained styling
        DataTable(
            data=data,
            css={
                &quot;table&quot;: &quot;w-full border-2 border-blue-500&quot;,
                &quot;header&quot;: &quot;px-4 py-3 bg-blue-600 text-white font-bold&quot;,
                &quot;cell&quot;: &quot;px-4 py-3 border border-blue-200&quot;,
                &quot;row&quot;: &quot;hover:bg-blue-50&quot;,
            }
        )
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    # Default CSS classes for sub-elements (matching original hardcoded values)
    _DEFAULT_ELEMENT_CSS = {
        &quot;header&quot;: &quot;px-3 py-2 bg-gray-400 border border-gray-400 text-left font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider&quot;,
        &quot;cell&quot;: &quot;px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300&quot;,
        &quot;row&quot;: &quot;odd:bg-white even:bg-gray-100 hover:bg-gray-200 transition-colors&quot;,
    }

    def __init__(
        self,
        data: list[dict] | Callable[[], list[dict]],
        columns: list[str] | Callable[[], list[str] | None] | None = None,
        id: str | None = None,
        css: str | Mapping[str, str | Callable[[], str]] | Callable[[], str | Mapping[str, str | Callable[[], str]]] | None = None,
        listen_to: str | list[str] | None = None,
        **attrs,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize a DataTable component.

        Args:
            data: Tabular data as list of dictionaries, or callable returning such list.
                  Each dict represents a row, keys are column names.
            columns: Optional list of column names to display, in order, or callable returning such list or None.
                    If None, columns are extracted from the first row&#x27;s keys.
                    Use this to control column order or select a subset of columns.
                    Can be a callable to enable dynamic column configuration based on state.
            id: HTML id attribute
            css: CSS styling. Can be:
                - str: Applied to the root &lt;table&gt; element (original behavior)
                - dict: Maps element types to CSS classes:
                    - &quot;table&quot;: Root &lt;table&gt; element
                    - &quot;header&quot;: &lt;th&gt; elements (header cells)
                    - &quot;row&quot;: &lt;tr&gt; elements (body rows)
                    - &quot;cell&quot;: &lt;td&gt; elements (body cells)
                - Callable: Returns either str or dict
            listen_to: State name to listen for changes (triggers re-render)
            **attrs: Additional HTML attributes (e.g., data-testid)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Don&#x27;t pass css to parent __init__ yet - we&#x27;ll handle it specially
        super().__init__(id=id, css=None, listen_to=listen_to, **attrs)
        self.data = data
        self.columns = columns
        self._raw_css = css

    def _resolve_css(self) -&gt; tuple[str, dict[str, str]]:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Resolve and normalize the CSS parameter.

        Returns:
            tuple: (root_css: str, element_css: dict[str, str])
                - root_css: CSS classes for the root &lt;table&gt; element
                - element_css: Dictionary of CSS classes for sub-elements (header, row, cell)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        css = self._raw_css

        # Handle callable
        if callable(css):
            css = css()

        # Handle None
        if css is None:
            return &quot;&quot;, {}

        # Handle string - applies to root table element
        if isinstance(css, str):
            return css, {}

        # Handle dict - can contain &#x27;table&#x27;, &#x27;header&#x27;, &#x27;row&#x27;, &#x27;cell&#x27; keys
        if isinstance(css, dict):
            resolved_dict = {}
            root_css = &quot;&quot;
            for key, value in css.items():
                if callable(value):
                    value = value()
                if value is None:
                    value = &quot;&quot;

                # &#x27;table&#x27; key applies to root element
                if key == &quot;table&quot;:
                    root_css = value
                else:
                    # Other keys apply to sub-elements
                    resolved_dict[key] = value
            return root_css, resolved_dict

        # Fallback
        return &quot;&quot;, {}

    def _get_columns(self, resolved_data: list[dict]) -&gt; list[str]:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the list of columns to display.

        If columns parameter was provided, use it.
        Otherwise, extract from first row&#x27;s keys.
        Returns empty list if data is empty.
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if self.columns is not None:
            resolved = self.columns() if callable(self.columns) else self.columns
            if resolved is not None:
                return resolved
        if resolved_data:
            return list(resolved_data[0].keys())
        return []

    def _get_value(self, row: dict, column: str) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the string value for a cell, handling None and missing keys.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        value = row.get(column, &quot;&quot;)
        if value is None:
            return &quot;&quot;
        return str(value)

    def _render_table(self, resolved_data: list[dict], element_css: dict[str, str]) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the HTML table structure with resolved data and CSS.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        columns = self._get_columns(resolved_data)

        # Merge user-provided CSS with defaults
        header_css = element_css.get(&quot;header&quot;, self._DEFAULT_ELEMENT_CSS[&quot;header&quot;])
        cell_css = element_css.get(&quot;cell&quot;, self._DEFAULT_ELEMENT_CSS[&quot;cell&quot;])
        row_css = element_css.get(&quot;row&quot;, self._DEFAULT_ELEMENT_CSS[&quot;row&quot;])

        # Render thead
        header_cells = &quot;&quot;.join(f&#x27;&lt;th class=&quot;{header_css}&quot;&gt;{col}&lt;/th&gt;&#x27; for col in columns)
        thead = f&quot;&lt;thead&gt;&lt;tr&gt;{header_cells}&lt;/tr&gt;&lt;/thead&gt;&quot;

        # Render tbody
        if not resolved_data:
            tbody = &quot;&lt;tbody&gt;&lt;/tbody&gt;&quot;
        else:
            rows_html = []
            for row in resolved_data:
                cells = &quot;&quot;.join(f&#x27;&lt;td class=&quot;{cell_css}&quot;&gt;{self._get_value(row, col)}&lt;/td&gt;&#x27; for col in columns)
                rows_html.append(f&#x27;&lt;tr class=&quot;{row_css}&quot;&gt;{cells}&lt;/tr&gt;&#x27;)
            tbody = f&quot;&lt;tbody&gt;{&#x27;&#x27;.join(rows_html)}&lt;/tbody&gt;&quot;

        return f&quot;{thead}{tbody}&quot;

    def _build_attrs(self, root_css: str, include_oob: bool = False) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Build HTML attributes string with optional OOB prefix.

        Args:
            root_css: CSS classes for the root table element
            include_oob: If True, prefix with hx-swap-oob=&quot;true&quot;

        Returns:
            Complete attributes string for &lt;table&gt; element
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        filtered_attrs = {}
        for k, v in self.attrs.items():
            if k != &quot;css&quot;:
                filtered_attrs[k] = self._resolve(v)

        # Add root CSS to class attribute
        if root_css:
            if &quot;class&quot; in filtered_attrs:
                filtered_attrs[&quot;class&quot;] += &quot; &quot; + root_css
            else:
                filtered_attrs[&quot;class&quot;] = root_css

        # Add id if present
        if self.id:
            filtered_attrs[&quot;id&quot;] = self.id

        attrs = &quot; &quot;.join(f&#x27;{k}=&quot;{v}&quot;&#x27; for k, v in filtered_attrs.items())

        if include_oob:
            attrs = f&#x27;hx-swap-oob=&quot;true&quot; {attrs}&#x27;.strip()

        return attrs

    def render(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the DataTable as HTML.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        resolved_data = self.data() if callable(self.data) else self.data
        root_css, element_css = self._resolve_css()
        attrs = self._build_attrs(root_css, include_oob=False)
        table_content = self._render_table(resolved_data, element_css)
        return f&quot;&lt;table {attrs}&gt;{table_content}&lt;/table&gt;&quot;

    def update(self) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render with hx-swap-oob for HTMX out-of-band updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if not self.id:
            return self.render()

        resolved_data = self.data() if callable(self.data) else self.data
        root_css, element_css = self._resolve_css()
        attrs = self._build_attrs(root_css, include_oob=True)
        table_content = self._render_table(resolved_data, element_css)
        return f&quot;&lt;table {attrs}&gt;{table_content}&lt;/table&gt;&quot;
</doc><doc title="State" desc="Reactive State container with per-session isolation and auto-propagation of mutations">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Reactive state management for inguitive.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from __future__ import annotations

import contextvars
import uuid
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Generic, TypeVar

from inguitive.session import (
    _get_data_registry,
)

_T = TypeVar(&quot;_T&quot;)

_LISTENERS_PREFIX = &quot;__listeners__&quot;

_state_name_registry: dict[str, State] = {}

# Context variable to track mutated state keys during request handling
_mutated_states: contextvars.ContextVar[set[str]] = contextvars.ContextVar(&quot;mutated_states&quot;, default=set())


@contextmanager
def _track_mutations():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Context manager to track state mutations during handler execution.

    Use this to wrap trigger handler execution. All State.set() calls within
    the context will be recorded and can be retrieved via get_mutated_states().
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    token = _mutated_states.set(set())
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        _mutated_states.reset(token)


def _get_mutated_states() -&gt; set[str]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return set of state keys mutated during current request.

    Returns:
        Copy of the set of state keys that were mutated via State.set()
        within the current _track_mutations() context.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _mutated_states.get().copy()


def _get_state_by_name(name: str) -&gt; State | None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Look up a named State object from the global registry.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _state_name_registry.get(name)


class State(Generic[_T]):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Reactive state container with per-session isolation.

    State values and listener sets are stored in the per-session data_registry,
    so each user&#x27;s session maintains fully independent state. The State object
    itself is a module-level singleton used only as a handle — it holds no
    mutable runtime data after construction.

    Named states (State(value, &quot;my_state&quot;)) are fully session-isolated.
    Unnamed states fall back to per-object storage, which is acceptable for
    states not shared across components via listen_to.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, initial_value: _T, name: str = &quot;&quot;):
        self._initial_value = initial_value
        self.name = name
        self._key = name if name else f&quot;__anon_{uuid.uuid4().hex}&quot;
        if name:
            _state_name_registry[name] = self

    def get(self) -&gt; _T:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return the current value for the active session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return _get_data_registry().get(self._key, self._initial_value)  # type: ignore[no-any-return]

    def set(self, new_value: _T) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Write a new value into the active session&#x27;s data registry.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        _get_data_registry()[self._key] = new_value
        # Track mutation for auto-propagation in trigger handlers
        _mutated_states.get().add(self._key)

    @property
    def listeners(self) -&gt; set[str]:  # type: ignore[valid-type]
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return the set of component IDs listening to this state in the active session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        listeners_key = f&quot;{_LISTENERS_PREFIX}{self._key}&quot;
        data = _get_data_registry()
        if listeners_key not in data:
            data[listeners_key] = set()
        return data[listeners_key]  # type: ignore[no-any-return]

    def add_listener(self, component_id: str) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Register a component ID as a listener for the active session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        self.listeners.add(component_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    def remove_listener(self, component_id: str) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Remove a component ID from the listeners for the active session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        self.listeners.discard(component_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
</doc><doc title="Session" desc="Session dataclass, MemoryBackend, RedisBackend, and context-local session helpers">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Session management with pluggable backends for inguitive.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import time
import uuid
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from contextvars import ContextVar
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

# Type aliases
SessionId = str
SessionData = dict[str, Any]


@dataclass
class Session:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Represents a user session with isolated registries.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    session_id: SessionId
    component_registry: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    state_registry: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    data_registry: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    last_accessed: float = field(default_factory=lambda: time.time())

    def to_dict(self) -&gt; SessionData:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Serialize session data for storage.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return {
            &quot;session_id&quot;: self.session_id,
            &quot;data_registry&quot;: self.data_registry,
            &quot;last_accessed&quot;: self.last_accessed,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: SessionData) -&gt; Session:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Deserialize session data from storage.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return cls(
            session_id=data[&quot;session_id&quot;],
            component_registry={},
            state_registry={},
            data_registry=data.get(&quot;data_registry&quot;, {}),
            last_accessed=data.get(&quot;last_accessed&quot;, 0.0),
        )


class SessionBackend(ABC):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Abstract base class for session backends.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    @abstractmethod
    def get_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; Session | None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Retrieve a session by ID. Returns None if not found.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    def save_session(self, session: Session) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Save a session to the backend.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    def delete_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Delete a session from the backend.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    def cleanup_expired(self) -&gt; int:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Clean up expired sessions. Returns number of sessions deleted.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        ...


class MemoryBackend(SessionBackend):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;In-memory session backend for development. Not suitable for production.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 3600):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize memory backend.

        Args:
            ttl_seconds: Session timeout in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour).
                        Sessions older than this will be cleaned up.
                        Set to 0 or negative for no expiry (not recommended).
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        self._sessions: dict[SessionId, Session] = {}
        self._ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds

    def get_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; Session | None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Retrieve session from memory.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        session = self._sessions.get(session_id)
        if session is None:
            return None
        # Update last_accessed timestamp on every access
        session.last_accessed = time.time()
        return session

    def save_session(self, session: Session) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Save session to memory.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        # Update last_accessed timestamp on every save
        session.last_accessed = time.time()
        self._sessions[session.session_id] = session

    def delete_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Delete session from memory.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        self._sessions.pop(session_id, None)

    def cleanup_expired(self) -&gt; int:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Clean up expired sessions.

        Removes all sessions that have not been accessed within the TTL period.
        Returns the number of sessions deleted.

        Note: If ttl_seconds is 0 or negative, no sessions are cleaned up.
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        if self._ttl_seconds &lt;= 0:
            # No expiry configured
            return 0

        current_time = time.time()
        expiry_threshold = current_time - self._ttl_seconds

        # Collect expired session IDs
        expired_ids = [
            session_id for session_id, session in self._sessions.items()
            if session.last_accessed &lt; expiry_threshold
        ]

        # Delete expired sessions
        for session_id in expired_ids:
            del self._sessions[session_id]

        return len(expired_ids)


class RedisBackend(SessionBackend):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Redis-based session backend for production.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(self, redis_url: str = &quot;redis://localhost:6379&quot;, ttl_seconds: int = 3600, db: int = 0):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        Initialize Redis backend.

        Args:
            redis_url: Redis connection URL
            ttl_seconds: Session timeout in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour)
            db: Redis database number
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        self._redis_url = redis_url
        self._ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds
        self._db = db
        self._client = None

    def _get_client(self):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Lazy initialization of Redis client.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if self._client is None:
            try:
                import redis

                self._client = redis.Redis.from_url(self._redis_url, db=self._db, decode_responses=True)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError(&quot;Redis backend requires &#x27;redis&#x27; package. Install with: pip install redis&quot;)
        return self._client

    def _make_key(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Create Redis key for session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return f&quot;inguitive:session:{session_id}&quot;

    def get_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; Session | None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Retrieve session from Redis.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        client = self._get_client()
        key = self._make_key(session_id)
        data = client.get(key)
        if data is None:
            return None
        try:
            session_data = json.loads(data)
            return Session.from_dict(session_data)
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):
            # Log error and return None
            return None

    def save_session(self, session: Session) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Save session to Redis with TTL.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        client = self._get_client()
        key = self._make_key(session.session_id)
        data = json.dumps(session.to_dict())
        client.setex(key, self._ttl_seconds, data)

    def delete_session(self, session_id: SessionId) -&gt; None:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Delete session from Redis.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        client = self._get_client()
        key = self._make_key(session_id)
        client.delete(key)

    def cleanup_expired(self) -&gt; int:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Redis handles TTL automatically. This is a no-op.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return 0


# Global session backend instance
_session_backend: SessionBackend | None = None

# Context variable for current session ID
_current_session_id: ContextVar[SessionId | None] = ContextVar(&quot;current_session_id&quot;, default=None)


def get_session_backend() -&gt; SessionBackend:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the configured session backend. Defaults to MemoryBackend.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    global _session_backend
    if _session_backend is None:
        _session_backend = MemoryBackend()
    return _session_backend


def set_session_backend(backend: SessionBackend) -&gt; None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Set the session backend. Call this during app initialization.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    global _session_backend
    _session_backend = backend


def _create_session() -&gt; Session:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Create a new session with unique ID.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    session_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    return Session(session_id=session_id)


def _get_current_session() -&gt; Session | None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the current session for this request/context.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    session_id = _current_session_id.get()
    if session_id is None:
        return None
    backend = get_session_backend()
    return backend.get_session(session_id)


def _get_or_create_current_session() -&gt; Session:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get current session or create a new one.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    session = _get_current_session()
    if session is not None:
        return session

    # Create new session
    session = _create_session()
    backend = get_session_backend()
    backend.save_session(session)

    # Set in context
    _current_session_id.set(session.session_id)
    return session


def _set_current_session(session: Session) -&gt; None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Set the current session for this request/context.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    _current_session_id.set(session.session_id)


def _clear_current_session() -&gt; None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Clear the current session from context.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    _current_session_id.set(None)


def get_session_id() -&gt; str | None:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the current session ID, or None if no session is active.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    session = _get_current_session()
    return session.session_id if session else None


# Convenience functions for registries
def _get_component_registry() -&gt; dict[str, Any]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the component registry for the current session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _get_or_create_current_session().component_registry


def _get_state_registry() -&gt; dict[str, Any]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the state registry for the current session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _get_or_create_current_session().state_registry


def _get_data_registry() -&gt; dict[str, Any]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get the data registry for the current session.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _get_or_create_current_session().data_registry
</doc><doc title="FastAPI integration" desc="create_app(), @app.page, @app.trigger_handler, SessionMiddleware">&quot;&quot;&quot;
FastAPI integration for inguitive.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from __future__ import annotations

import inspect
import uuid
from collections.abc import Callable
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, ParamSpec, Protocol, TypeVar, runtime_checkable

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse
from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates

from inguitive.htmx import update_components
from inguitive.session import (
    Session,
    SessionBackend,
    _clear_current_session,
    _set_current_session,
    get_session_backend,
    set_session_backend,
)
from inguitive.state import (
    _get_mutated_states,
    _get_state_by_name,
    _track_mutations,
)
from inguitive.trigger import _trigger_args_context

# Type variables for decorator type annotations
_P = ParamSpec(&quot;_P&quot;)
_T = TypeVar(&quot;_T&quot;)

# Type aliases for decorator return types
_TriggerDecorator = Callable[[Callable[_P, _T]], Callable[_P, _T]]
_PageDecorator = Callable[[str | None], Callable[[Callable[_P, _T]], Callable[_P, _T]]]


@runtime_checkable
class InguitiveApp(Protocol[_P, _T]):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Protocol describing an inguitive application with custom decorators.

    This Protocol extends the FastAPI instance with inguitive-specific decorators.
    Type checkers will recognize these custom attributes on objects of this type.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    # Custom decorators
    trigger_handler: _TriggerDecorator[_P, _T]
    page: _PageDecorator[_P, _T]


def _register_page_route(app, path: str, handler: Callable[_P, _T]):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Helper to register a page route on an app.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    @app.get(path, response_class=HTMLResponse)
    async def route_wrapper(request: Request, h=handler):
        sig = inspect.signature(h)
        needs_request = &quot;request&quot; in sig.parameters
        needs_form_data = &quot;form_data&quot; in sig.parameters
        is_async = inspect.iscoroutinefunction(h)

        kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
        if needs_request:
            kwargs[&quot;request&quot;] = request
        if needs_form_data:
            form_data_dict = dict(await request.form())
            kwargs[&quot;form_data&quot;] = form_data_dict

        result = await h(**kwargs) if is_async else h(**kwargs)

        # If result is a Response object (e.g., RedirectResponse), return it directly
        from starlette.responses import Response

        if isinstance(result, Response):
            return result

        # Auto-render Components if they have a render method
        if hasattr(result, &quot;render&quot;) and callable(result.render):
            content = result.render()
        else:
            content = str(result)

        # Wrap in base template
        templates = app.state.templates
        return templates.TemplateResponse(request, &quot;base.html&quot;, {&quot;content&quot;: content})


def _register_trigger_route(app, trigger_name: str, handler: Callable):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Helper to register a trigger route on an app.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    @app.post(f&quot;/_trigger/{trigger_name.lstrip(&#x27;/&#x27;)}&quot;, response_class=HTMLResponse)
    async def route_wrapper(request: Request, h=handler, tn=trigger_name):
        sig = inspect.signature(h)
        needs_request = &quot;request&quot; in sig.parameters
        needs_form_data = &quot;form_data&quot; in sig.parameters
        is_async = inspect.iscoroutinefunction(h)

        kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
        if needs_request:
            kwargs[&quot;request&quot;] = request

        # Extract query params which contain trigger_args from Component
        query_params = dict(request.query_params)

        # Track state mutations during handler execution for auto-propagation
        with _track_mutations():
            # Set trigger_args in context for get_trigger_args() access
            with _trigger_args_context(query_params):
                if needs_form_data:
                    form_data_dict = dict(await request.form())
                    # Merge query parameters (from trigger_args) into form_data
                    form_data_dict.update(query_params)
                    kwargs[&quot;form_data&quot;] = form_data_dict

                result = await h(**kwargs) if is_async else h(**kwargs)

                # If handler returned explicit response, use it (allows overriding auto-propagation)
                if result:
                    return result

                # Otherwise, auto-generate OOB response from mutated states
                mutated_state_keys = _get_mutated_states()
                all_component_ids: set[str] = set()
                for state_key in mutated_state_keys:
                    # Get the State object for this key and collect its listeners
                    state = _get_state_by_name(state_key)
                    if state is not None:
                        all_component_ids.update(state.listeners)

                return update_components(*all_component_ids)


class SessionMiddleware:
