    &quot;&quot;&quot;FastAPI/Starlette ASGI middleware for session management.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def __init__(
        self,
        app,
        session_cookie_name: str = &quot;inguitive_session_id&quot;,
        session_cookie_max_age: int = 3600,
        session_cookie_secure: bool = False,
        session_cookie_httponly: bool = True,
        cleanup_interval: int = 100,
    ):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Initialize SessionMiddleware.

        Args:
            app: The ASGI application
            session_cookie_name: Name of the session cookie
            session_cookie_max_age: Cookie max age in seconds
            session_cookie_secure: Whether cookie is secure (HTTPS only)
            session_cookie_httponly: Whether cookie is HTTP-only
            cleanup_interval: Call cleanup_expired() every N requests (default: 100)
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        self.app = app
        self.session_cookie_name = session_cookie_name
        self.session_cookie_max_age = session_cookie_max_age
        self.session_cookie_secure = session_cookie_secure
        self.session_cookie_httponly = session_cookie_httponly
        self.cleanup_interval = cleanup_interval
        self._request_count = 0

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Process ASGI request with session management.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if scope[&quot;type&quot;] not in (&quot;http&quot;, &quot;websocket&quot;):
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        # Periodic cleanup of expired sessions
        self._request_count += 1
        if self._request_count % self.cleanup_interval == 0:
            backend = get_session_backend()
            backend.cleanup_expired()

        # Extract cookies from headers
        headers = dict(scope.get(&quot;headers&quot;, []))
        cookie_header = headers.get(b&quot;cookie&quot;, b&quot;&quot;).decode(&quot;latin-1&quot;)
        cookies = {}
        for part in cookie_header.split(&quot;;&quot;):
            part = part.strip()
            if &quot;=&quot; in part:
                k, v = part.split(&quot;=&quot;, 1)
                cookies[k.strip()] = v.strip()

        session_id = cookies.get(self.session_cookie_name)
        backend = get_session_backend()

        if session_id:
            session = backend.get_session(session_id)
            if session is None:
                session = Session(session_id=session_id)
                backend.save_session(session)
        else:
            session = Session(session_id=str(uuid.uuid4()))
            backend.save_session(session)

        _set_current_session(session)

        async def send_with_cookie(message):
            if message[&quot;type&quot;] == &quot;http.response.start&quot;:
                headers_list = list(message.get(&quot;headers&quot;, []))
                cookie_value = (
                    f&quot;{self.session_cookie_name}={session.session_id}; &quot;
                    f&quot;Max-Age={self.session_cookie_max_age}; Path=/; SameSite=Lax&quot;
                )
                if self.session_cookie_httponly:
                    cookie_value += &quot;; HttpOnly&quot;
                if self.session_cookie_secure:
                    cookie_value += &quot;; Secure&quot;
                headers_list.append((b&quot;set-cookie&quot;, cookie_value.encode(&quot;latin-1&quot;)))
                message = dict(message, headers=headers_list)
            await send(message)

        try:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_cookie)
        finally:
            backend.save_session(session)
            _clear_current_session()


def create_app(
    template_dir: str | Path = &quot;templates&quot;,
    session_backend: SessionBackend | None = None,
    session_cookie_name: str = &quot;inguitive_session_id&quot;,
    session_cookie_max_age: int = 3600,
    session_cookie_secure: bool = False,
    session_cookie_httponly: bool = True,
    session_cleanup_interval: int = 100,
) -&gt; InguitiveApp[_P, _T]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Create and configure a FastAPI application for inguitive.

    Args:
        template_dir: Directory containing Jinja2 templates
        session_backend: Session backend to use (defaults to MemoryBackend)
        session_cookie_name: Name of the session cookie
        session_cookie_max_age: Cookie max age in seconds
        session_cookie_secure: Whether cookie is secure (HTTPS only)
        session_cookie_httponly: Whether cookie is HTTP-only
        session_cleanup_interval: Call cleanup_expired() every N requests (default: 100)

    Returns:
        InguitiveApp - the FastAPI application with inguitive decorators
        (trigger_handler and page) and templates accessible via app.state.templates
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    app = FastAPI()
    templates = Jinja2Templates(directory=template_dir)
    app.state.templates = templates

    # Initialize per-app storage for handlers
    app.state.trigger_handlers = {}
    app.state.page_routes = {}

    # Add app-scoped decorator methods
    def _page_decorator(path: str | None = None):
        def decorator(func: Callable):
            actual_path = path if path is not None else &quot;/&quot;
            app.state.page_routes[actual_path] = func
            _register_page_route(app, actual_path, func)
            return func

        return decorator

    def _trigger_decorator(trigger_name: str | None | Callable = None):
        if callable(trigger_name):
            # Called as @app.trigger_handler (without parentheses)
            # trigger_name is actually the function
            func = trigger_name
            actual_trigger_name = func.__name__
            app.state.trigger_handlers[actual_trigger_name] = func
            _register_trigger_route(app, actual_trigger_name, func)
            return func
        else:
            # Called as @app.trigger_handler(&quot;name&quot;) (with parentheses)
            # trigger_name is the name string
            def decorator(func: Callable):
                actual_trigger_name = trigger_name or func.__name__
                app.state.trigger_handlers[actual_trigger_name] = func
                _register_trigger_route(app, actual_trigger_name, func)
                return func

            return decorator

    # Attach decorator methods to app
    app.page = _page_decorator  # type: ignore
    app.trigger_handler = _trigger_decorator  # type: ignore

    # Configure session backend
    if session_backend is not None:
        set_session_backend(session_backend)

    # Add session middleware
    app.add_middleware(
        SessionMiddleware,
        session_cookie_name=session_cookie_name,
        session_cookie_max_age=session_cookie_max_age,
        session_cookie_secure=session_cookie_secure,
        session_cookie_httponly=session_cookie_httponly,
        cleanup_interval=session_cleanup_interval,
    )

    return app  # type: ignore[return-value]


def run_app(app_module: str = &quot;app:app&quot;, host: str = &quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port: int = 8000, reload: bool = True):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Run the FastAPI application using Uvicorn.

    Args:
        app_module: Uvicorn app module string (e.g., &quot;app:app&quot;)
        host: Host to bind to
        port: Port to bind to
        reload: Enable auto-reload in development
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(app_module, host=host, port=port, reload=reload)


def redirect(url: str, status_code: int = 302):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Perform an HTTP redirect to the specified URL.

    Args:
        url: The URL to redirect to
        status_code: HTTP status code (302 for temporary redirect, 301 for permanent)

    Returns:
        RedirectResponse: FastAPI redirect response
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse

    return RedirectResponse(url=url, status_code=status_code)
</doc><doc title="HTMX helpers" desc="update_components() — renders components as OOB HTML for partial page updates">&quot;&quot;&quot;
HTMX helper functions for inguitive.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from inguitive.session import _get_component_registry


def update_components(*component_ids: str) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render multiple components as OOB HTML for HTMX updates.

    Args:
        *component_ids: Component IDs to update

    Returns:
        Concatenated HTML string with hx-swap-oob attributes
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    html_parts: list[str] = []
    component_registry = _get_component_registry()
    for cid in component_ids:
        if cid in component_registry:
            html_parts.append(component_registry[cid].update())
    return &quot;&quot;.join(html_parts)
</doc><doc title="Trigger" desc="get_trigger_args() — access trigger_args dict inside a trigger handler">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Trigger argument context management for inguitive framework.

Provides context-local storage for trigger_args passed from Components to trigger handlers,
allowing handlers to access these arguments without requiring form_data or request parameters.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from contextlib import contextmanager
from contextvars import ContextVar

# Context variable to store trigger_args for current request
_trigger_args_var: ContextVar[dict[str, str]] = ContextVar(
    &quot;trigger_args&quot;,
    default={},
)


def get_trigger_args() -&gt; dict[str, str]:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Get trigger_args dictionary for the current trigger handler execution.

    Returns the dictionary of trigger arguments that were passed via Component&#x27;s
    trigger_args parameter. Returns an empty dict if no trigger_args were passed
    or if called outside of a trigger handler context.

    Example:
        @app.trigger_handler
        def my_handler():
            args = get_trigger_args()
            column = args.get(&quot;column&quot;)

    Returns:
        Dict[str, str]: The trigger arguments dictionary.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    return _trigger_args_var.get()


@contextmanager
def _trigger_args_context(args: dict[str, str]):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Context manager to temporarily set trigger_args for handler execution.

    This is used internally by the framework to populate trigger_args from URL
    query parameters before calling a trigger handler, and to clean up
    afterwards.

    Args:
        args: Dictionary of trigger argument key-value pairs.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    token = _trigger_args_var.set(args.copy())
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        _trigger_args_var.reset(token)
</doc><doc title="Utils" desc="nl2br() and other general helpers">&quot;&quot;&quot;Utility functions for inguitive framework.&quot;&quot;&quot;


def nl2br(text: str | None) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Convert newline characters to HTML line break tags.

    Args:
        text: Input string potentially containing newline characters.
        May be None, which returns an empty string.

    Returns:
        String with all newline characters replaced by &lt;br&gt; tags.
        None input returns empty string.

    Example:
        &gt;&gt;&gt; nl2br(&quot;Line 1\nLine 2&quot;)
        &#x27;Line 1&lt;br&gt;Line 2&#x27;

        &gt;&gt;&gt; nl2br(&quot;Hello\n\nWorld&quot;)
        &#x27;Hello&lt;br&gt;&lt;br&gt;World&#x27;

        &gt;&gt;&gt; nl2br(None)
        &#x27;&#x27;
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    if text is None:
        return &quot;&quot;
    return text.replace(&quot;\n&quot;, &quot;&lt;br&gt;&quot;)
</doc><doc title="Counter app" desc="Per-session counter with theme toggle — demonstrates State, listen_to, and dynamic CSS">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Counter example application using inguitive framework.

Run with: uvicorn examples.counter_app:app --reload

Per-Session Isolation Demonstration
-----------------------------------
This example demonstrates inguitive&#x27;s per-session state isolation.
Each browser session maintains its own independent counter and theme state.

To test:
1. Open this app in one regular browser window and one incognito/private window
2. Note the unique Session ID displayed in each window
3. Increment the counter in Window 1 - Window 2&#x27;s counter remains unchanged
4. Toggle theme in Window 1 - Window 2&#x27;s theme remains unchanged

This proves that State values are fully isolated per user session.
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import Button, Div, Icon, State, Text, create_app, get_session_id, update_components
from inguitive.svg import MOON, SUN

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)


# --- State Instances ---
counter_state = State(0, &quot;counter_state&quot;)
theme_state = State(&quot;light&quot;, &quot;theme_state&quot;)


# --- CSS ---
COLOR_BASE = &quot;slate&quot;
COLOR_100 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-100&quot;
COLOR_300 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-300&quot;
COLOR_400 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-400&quot;
COLOR_600 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-600&quot;
COLOR_900 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-900&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_1 = &quot;blue-700&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2 = &quot;fuchsia-600&quot;
BUTTON_SHAPE = &quot;p-3 rounded-md font-semibold cursor-pointer shadow-lg active:shadow-none&quot;
BUTTON_PRIMARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_BRAND_1} to-{COLOR_BRAND_2} text-{COLOR_100}&quot;
BUTTON_SECONDARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_400} to-{COLOR_300} text-{COLOR_900}&quot;


# --- Trigger Handlers ---
@app.trigger_handler
def increment():
    counter_state.set(counter_state.get() + 1)
    return update_components(*counter_state.listeners)


@app.trigger_handler
def reset():
    counter_state.set(0)
    return update_components(*counter_state.listeners)


@app.trigger_handler
def toggle_theme():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Toggle between light and dark theme.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current: str = theme_state.get()
    new_theme: str = &quot;dark&quot; if current == &quot;light&quot; else &quot;light&quot;
    theme_state.set(new_theme)
    return update_components(*theme_state.listeners)


# --- Counter Component ---
def Counter() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Main counter component demonstrating inguitive features.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def dynamic_icon() -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Dynamic icon based on theme state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return MOON if theme_state.get() == &quot;light&quot; else SUN

    def dynamic_text() -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Dynamic text based on counter state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        return f&quot;Count: {counter_state.get()}&quot;

    def dynamic_text_css() -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Dynamic styling based on counter value.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        count = counter_state.get()
        base = &quot;text-xl text-center&quot;
        if count &gt; 5:
            return f&quot;{base} text-red-500 font-bold&quot;
        return f&quot;{base} text-{COLOR_900}&quot;

    def dynamic_div_css() -&gt; str:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Dynamic styling based on theme state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        bg = f&quot;bg-{COLOR_100}&quot; if theme_state.get() == &quot;light&quot; else f&quot;bg-{COLOR_900}&quot;
        return f&quot;{bg} w-full min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center&quot;

    return Div(
        Div(
            Div(
                Button(
                    Icon(
                        dynamic_icon,
                        css=&quot;w-6 h-6&quot;,
                    ),
                    trigger=&quot;toggle_theme&quot;,
                    id=&quot;theme-toggle&quot;,
                    css=BUTTON_PRIMARY,
                ),
                css=&quot;w-full flex justify-end&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                text=dynamic_text,
                id=&quot;counter-label&quot;,
                css=dynamic_text_css,
                listen_to=&quot;counter_state&quot;,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;+1&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;increment&quot;,
                css=f&quot;{BUTTON_PRIMARY} w-full&quot;,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Reset&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;reset&quot;,
                css=f&quot;{BUTTON_SECONDARY} w-full&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                f&quot;Session: {get_session_id()}&quot;,
                css=f&quot;text-sm text-center text-{COLOR_600}&quot;,
            ),
            id=&quot;counter-card&quot;,
            css=&quot;overflow-hidden rounded-xl bg-white shadow-lg p-6 space-y-6 w-sm&quot;,
        ),
        id=&quot;theme-container&quot;,
        css=dynamic_div_css,
        listen_to=&quot;theme_state&quot;,
    )


# --- Routes ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def home():
    return Counter()


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.counter_app:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc><doc title="Todo app" desc="CRUD with filtering and real-time count — demonstrates list state and conditional rendering">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Todo App example using inguitive framework.

Demonstrates CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) with reactive state management.

Features:
- Add new todos
- Mark todos as complete/incomplete
- Delete todos
- Filter by status (All/Active/Completed)
- Clear all completed todos
- Real-time count of remaining items
- Session isolation (each browser tab has independent state)

Run with: uvicorn examples.todo_app:app --reload
&quot;&quot;&quot;

import uuid
from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import (
    Button,
    Checkbox,
    Div,
    Form,
    Input,
    State,
    Text,
    create_app,
    update_components,
)

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)


# --- State Instances ---
# Single state for all todo data: {&quot;todos&quot;: [...], &quot;filter&quot;: &quot;all&quot; | &quot;active&quot; | &quot;completed&quot;}
todo_state = State({&quot;todos&quot;: [], &quot;filter&quot;: &quot;all&quot;}, &quot;todo_state&quot;)


# --- CSS ---
COLOR_PRIMARY = &quot;slate-600&quot;
COLOR_SECONDARY = &quot;slate-300&quot;
COLOR_ACTIVE = &quot;amber-300&quot;
BUTTON_SHAPE = &quot;px-3 py-2 rounded-md font-semibold cursor-pointer&quot;


# --- Trigger Handlers ---
@app.trigger_handler
async def add_todo(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Add a new todo item to the list.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = todo_state.get()
    new_todo = {
        &quot;id&quot;: str(uuid.uuid4()),
        &quot;title&quot;: form_data.get(&quot;title&quot;, &quot;&quot;).strip(),
        &quot;completed&quot;: False,
    }
    # Only add if title is not empty
    if new_todo[&quot;title&quot;]:
        current[&quot;todos&quot;].append(new_todo)
        todo_state.set(current)
        return update_components(*todo_state.listeners)
    return &quot;&quot;


@app.trigger_handler
async def toggle_todo(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Toggle the completed status of a todo.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = todo_state.get()
    todo_id = form_data.get(&quot;id&quot;)
    if todo_id:
        for todo in current[&quot;todos&quot;]:
            if todo[&quot;id&quot;] == todo_id:
                todo[&quot;completed&quot;] = not todo[&quot;completed&quot;]
                break
        todo_state.set(current)
        return update_components(*todo_state.listeners)
    return &quot;&quot;


@app.trigger_handler
async def delete_todo(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Delete a todo item from the list.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = todo_state.get()
    todo_id = form_data.get(&quot;id&quot;)
    if todo_id:
        current[&quot;todos&quot;] = [t for t in current[&quot;todos&quot;] if t[&quot;id&quot;] != todo_id]
        todo_state.set(current)
        return update_components(*todo_state.listeners)
    return &quot;&quot;


@app.trigger_handler
async def set_filter(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Set the filter for the todo list (all/active/completed).&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = todo_state.get()
    new_filter = form_data.get(&quot;filter&quot;, &quot;all&quot;)
    if new_filter in (&quot;all&quot;, &quot;active&quot;, &quot;completed&quot;):
        current[&quot;filter&quot;] = new_filter
        todo_state.set(current)
        return update_components(*todo_state.listeners)
    return &quot;&quot;


@app.trigger_handler
async def clear_completed(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Remove all completed todos from the list.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = todo_state.get()
    current[&quot;todos&quot;] = [t for t in current[&quot;todos&quot;] if not t[&quot;completed&quot;]]
    todo_state.set(current)
    return update_components(*todo_state.listeners)


# --- Component Functions ---


def TodoItem(todo: dict) -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render a single todo item with checkbox, title, and delete button.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def todo_checked():
        return todo[&quot;completed&quot;]

    def todo_title():
        return todo[&quot;title&quot;]

    def todo_styles():
        return &quot;flex-1&quot; + (&quot; line-through&quot; if todo[&quot;completed&quot;] else &quot;&quot;)

    def todo_div_css():
        base = f&quot;flex items-center gap-3 p-3 border-b border-{COLOR_SECONDARY} last:border-b-0&quot;
        if todo[&quot;completed&quot;]:
            return f&quot;{base} bg-green-300&quot;
        return base

    return Div(
        Checkbox(
            id=f&quot;todo-{todo[&#x27;id&#x27;]}&quot;,
            checked=todo_checked,
            trigger=&quot;toggle_todo&quot;,
            trigger_args={&quot;id&quot;: todo[&quot;id&quot;]},
            name=&quot;completed&quot;,
            css=&quot;h-5 w-5 cursor-pointer&quot;,
        ),
        Text(
            todo_title,
            css=todo_styles,
        ),
        Button(
            &quot;Delete&quot;,
            trigger=&quot;delete_todo&quot;,
            trigger_args={&quot;id&quot;: todo[&quot;id&quot;]},
            css=&quot;underline cursor-pointer&quot;,
        ),
        css=todo_div_css,
    )


def TodoForm() -&gt; Form:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Form for adding new todos.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Form(
        Input(
            id=&quot;todo-input&quot;,
            name=&quot;title&quot;,
            placeholder=&quot;What needs to be done?&quot;,
            css=f&quot;flex-1 p-2 border border-{COLOR_SECONDARY} rounded-md&quot;,
        ),
        Button(
            &quot;Add&quot;,
            type=&quot;submit&quot;,
            css=f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_PRIMARY} text-white&quot;,
        ),
        trigger=&quot;add_todo&quot;,
        css=&quot;flex gap-3&quot;,
    )


def TodoList() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the list of todos based on the current filter.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def todo_list_content() -&gt; list:
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return list of todo item components based on current state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        data = todo_state.get()
        todos = data[&quot;todos&quot;]
        filter_type = data[&quot;filter&quot;]

        # Filter todos based on current filter
        filtered_todos = {
            &quot;all&quot;: todos,
            &quot;active&quot;: [t for t in todos if not t[&quot;completed&quot;]],
            &quot;completed&quot;: [t for t in todos if t[&quot;completed&quot;]],
        }.get(filter_type, todos)

        if not filtered_todos:
            return [Text(&quot;No todos found.&quot;, css=&quot;p-3 text-center&quot;)]

        return [TodoItem(todo) for todo in filtered_todos]

    return Div(
        todo_list_content,
        id=&quot;todo_list&quot;,
        listen_to=&quot;todo_state&quot;,
        css=f&quot;border border-{COLOR_SECONDARY} rounded-md overflow-hidden&quot;,
    )


def TodoFilters() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Filter controls for the todo list.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def filter_all_css():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return CSS classes for All filter button with active state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        current_filter = todo_state.get()[&quot;filter&quot;]
        if current_filter == &quot;all&quot;:
            return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_ACTIVE}&quot;
        return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_SECONDARY}&quot;

    def filter_active_css():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return CSS classes for Active filter button with active state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        current_filter = todo_state.get()[&quot;filter&quot;]
        if current_filter == &quot;active&quot;:
            return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_ACTIVE}&quot;
        return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_SECONDARY}&quot;

    def filter_completed_css():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return CSS classes for Completed filter button with active state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        current_filter = todo_state.get()[&quot;filter&quot;]
        if current_filter == &quot;completed&quot;:
            return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_ACTIVE}&quot;
        return f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_SECONDARY}&quot;

    return Div(
        Button(
            &quot;All&quot;,
            trigger=&quot;set_filter&quot;,
            trigger_args={&quot;filter&quot;: &quot;all&quot;},
            css=filter_all_css,
            listen_to=&quot;todo_state&quot;,
        ),
        Button(
            &quot;Active&quot;,
            id=&quot;active_filter_button&quot;,
            trigger=&quot;set_filter&quot;,
            trigger_args={&quot;filter&quot;: &quot;active&quot;},
            css=filter_active_css,
            listen_to=&quot;todo_state&quot;,
        ),
        Button(
            &quot;Completed&quot;,
            trigger=&quot;set_filter&quot;,
            trigger_args={&quot;filter&quot;: &quot;completed&quot;},
            css=filter_completed_css,
            listen_to=&quot;todo_state&quot;,
        ),
        css=&quot;grid grid-cols-3 gap-x-3&quot;,
        id=&quot;filter_buttons&quot;,
    )


def TodoCount() -&gt; Text:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Display the count of remaining active todos.&quot;&quot;&quot;

    def todo_count_text() -&gt; str:
        todos = todo_state.get()[&quot;todos&quot;]
        active_count = sum(1 for t in todos if not t[&quot;completed&quot;])
        item_word = &quot;item&quot; if active_count == 1 else &quot;items&quot;
        return f&quot;{active_count} {item_word} left&quot;

    return Text(
        todo_count_text,
        id=&quot;todo_count&quot;,
        listen_to=&quot;todo_state&quot;,
    )


def TodoApp() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Main todo application component.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Div(
            Text(&quot;Todo App&quot;, css=&quot;text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-center&quot;),
            TodoForm(),
            TodoFilters(),
            TodoCount(),
            TodoList(),
            Div(
                Button(
                    &quot;Clear Completed&quot;,
                    trigger=&quot;clear_completed&quot;,
                    css=f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-{COLOR_SECONDARY}&quot;,
                ),
                css=&quot;flex justify-center&quot;,
            ),
            id=&quot;todo_container&quot;,
            css=&quot;max-w-md mx-auto p-6 bg-white rounded-xl shadow-md space-y-6 w-full&quot;,
        ),
        css=&quot;w-full min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-slate-100&quot;,
    )


# --- Routes ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def home():
    return TodoApp()


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.todo_app:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc><doc title="Chat app" desc="Real-time chat — demonstrates shared state and append patterns">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Chat App example using inguitive framework.

Demonstrates:
- Real-time chat interface with bot responses
- State management for chat history
- Form handling with trigger handlers

Features:
- User can type questions
- Bot responds with funny answers
- Chat history persists during the session

Run with: uvicorn examples.chat_app:app --reload
&quot;&quot;&quot;

import random
from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import Button, Div, Form, Icon, Input, State, Text, create_app, update_components

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)


# --- CSS ---
COLOR_BASE = &quot;slate&quot;
COLOR_100 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-100&quot;
COLOR_900 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-900&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_1 = &quot;blue-700&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2 = &quot;fuchsia-600&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT = &quot;fuchsia-500&quot;
BUTTON_SHAPE = &quot;p-3 rounded-md font-semibold cursor-pointer shadow-lg active:shadow-none&quot;
BUTTON_PRIMARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_BRAND_1} to-{COLOR_BRAND_2} text-{COLOR_100} hover:to-{COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT}&quot;


# --- Icons ---
ARROW_UP_ICON = f&quot;&quot;&quot;
&lt;svg class=&quot;w-6 h-6 text-{COLOR_100}&quot; aria-hidden=&quot;true&quot; xmlns=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2000/svg&quot; width=&quot;24&quot; height=&quot;24&quot; fill=&quot;none&quot; viewBox=&quot;0 0 24 24&quot;&gt;
  &lt;path stroke=&quot;currentColor&quot; stroke-linecap=&quot;round&quot; stroke-linejoin=&quot;round&quot; stroke-width=&quot;2&quot; d=&quot;M12 6v13m0-13 4 4m-4-4-4 4&quot;/&gt;
&lt;/svg&gt;
&quot;&quot;&quot;

USER_ICON = f&quot;&quot;&quot;
&lt;svg class=&quot;w-6 h-6 text-{COLOR_100}&quot; aria-hidden=&quot;true&quot; xmlns=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2000/svg&quot; width=&quot;24&quot; height=&quot;24&quot; fill=&quot;none&quot; viewBox=&quot;0 0 24 24&quot;&gt;
  &lt;path stroke=&quot;currentColor&quot; stroke-width=&quot;2&quot; d=&quot;M7 17v1a1 1 0 0 0 1 1h8a1 1 0 0 0 1-1v-1a3 3 0 0 0-3-3h-4a3 3 0 0 0-3 3Zm8-9a3 3 0 1 1-6 0 3 3 0 0 1 6 0Z&quot;/&gt;
&lt;/svg&gt;
&quot;&quot;&quot;

LIGHTBULB_ICON = f&quot;&quot;&quot;
&lt;svg class=&quot;w-6 h-6 text-{COLOR_100}&quot; aria-hidden=&quot;true&quot; xmlns=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2000/svg&quot; width=&quot;24&quot; height=&quot;24&quot; fill=&quot;none&quot; viewBox=&quot;0 0 24 24&quot;&gt;
  &lt;path stroke=&quot;currentColor&quot; stroke-linecap=&quot;round&quot; stroke-linejoin=&quot;round&quot; stroke-width=&quot;2&quot; d=&quot;M9 9a3 3 0 0 1 3-3m-2 15h4m0-3c0-4.1 4-4.9 4-9A6 6 0 1 0 6 9c0 4 4 5 4 9h4Z&quot;/&gt;
&lt;/svg&gt;
&quot;&quot;&quot;


# --- Bot Responses ---
answers = [
    &quot;I have no idea.&quot;,
    &quot;Sorry, I don&#x27;t know.&quot;,
    &quot;Who knows, who knows...&quot;,
    &quot;Great question! But can we talk about something else?&quot;,
    &quot;I&#x27;m not sure. Maybe you should ask your neighbor.&quot;,
    &quot;How am I supposed to know that?&quot;,
    &quot;I always say, &#x27;There are no stupid questions, only stupid answers.&#x27; But right now, I&#x27;m not so sure about that anymore...&quot;,
    &quot;Please ask me again tomorrow.&quot;,
    &quot;Could you repeat the question?&quot;,
    &quot;I don&#x27;t think there&#x27;s anyone out there who can answer that.&quot;,
]


# --- State Instance ---
# State to store chat history as list of [speaker, message] pairs
chat_history_state = State([[&quot;bot&quot;, &quot;Ask me anything!&quot;]], &quot;chat_history_state&quot;)


@app.trigger_handler
def generate_bot_response(form_data: dict):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Generate a bot response based on the user&#x27;s message.

    Args:
        form_data: Dictionary containing form submission data with &#x27;message&#x27; key

    Returns:
        str: HTMX update response for component listeners
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    user_message = form_data.get(&quot;message&quot;, &quot;&quot;).strip()
    if not user_message:
        return &quot;&quot;

    chat_history = chat_history_state.get()
    chat_history.append([&quot;user&quot;, user_message])
    if &quot;?&quot; in user_message:
        answer = random.choice(answers)
    else:
        answer = &quot;I don&#x27;t think that&#x27;s a question... Could you ask me a question?&quot;
    chat_history.append([&quot;bot&quot;, answer])
    chat_history_state.set(chat_history)
    return update_components(*chat_history_state.listeners)


# --- Components ---
def ChatBubble(speaker: str, message: str) -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Create a chat bubble for the given speaker and message.

    Args:
        speaker: Either &#x27;user&#x27; or &#x27;bot&#x27; to determine bubble styling
        message: The text content of the chat message

    Returns:
        Div: A styled chat bubble component with icon and message text
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    text_css = f&quot;bg-{COLOR_100} text-{COLOR_900} rounded-md p-3&quot;
    if speaker == &quot;user&quot;:
        return Div(
            Div(
                Div(
                    Icon(USER_ICON),
                    css=f&quot;rounded-full bg-{COLOR_BRAND_2} p-3&quot;,
                ),
                Text(
                    message,
                    css=text_css,
                ),
                css=&quot;flex justify-end items-start gap-3&quot;,
            ),
            css=&quot;w-full pl-30&quot;,
        )
    else:
        return Div(
            Div(
                Div(
                    Icon(LIGHTBULB_ICON),
                    css=f&quot;rounded-full bg-{COLOR_BRAND_1} p-3&quot;,
                ),
                Text(
                    message,
                    css=text_css,
                ),
                css=&quot;flex justify-start items-start gap-3&quot;,
            ),
            css=&quot;w-full pr-30&quot;,
        )


def ChatHistory() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Create the chat history display component.

    Renders all chat bubbles from the chat history state and updates
    when new messages are added.

    Returns:
        Div: A container with all chat bubble components
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def chat_bubbles():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Render chat bubbles from history state.

        We use reversed() to iterate the list in reverse order. Combined with the
        flex-col-reverse CSS on the parent ChatHistory Div, this ensures the latest
        messages appear at the bottom of the chat while maintaining efficient append
        operations when adding new messages.

        Returns:
            list: List of ChatBubble components in display order
        &quot;&quot;&quot;
        return [ChatBubble(speaker, message) for speaker, message in reversed(chat_history_state.get())]

    return Div(
        chat_bubbles,
        listen_to=&quot;chat_history_state&quot;,
        css=&quot;w-full flex flex-1 flex-col-reverse gap-6 overflow-y-auto&quot;,
    )


def MessageForm() -&gt; Form:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Create the chat message input form.

    Returns:
        Form: A form component with input field and submit button
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Form(
        Input(
            name=&quot;message&quot;,
            placeholder=&quot;Type your question here...&quot;,
            css=f&quot;flex-grow p-3 bg-{COLOR_100} rounded-md border&quot;,
        ),
        Button(
            Icon(ARROW_UP_ICON),
            type=&quot;submit&quot;,
            css=BUTTON_PRIMARY,
        ),
        trigger=&quot;generate_bot_response&quot;,
        css=&quot;w-full flex flex-row gap-3&quot;,
    )


# --- Routes ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def index():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the main chat application page.

    Returns:
        Div: The complete chat interface with history and input
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Div(
            Text(
                &quot;Welcome to the Chat App!&quot;,
                css=f&quot;text-2xl font-bold text-{COLOR_100} text-center&quot;,
            ),
            ChatHistory(),
            MessageForm(),
            css=&quot;w-full max-w-4xl h-screen mx-auto p-6 flex flex-col gap-12&quot;,
        ),
        css=f&quot;w-full bg-{COLOR_900}&quot;,
    )


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.chat_app:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc><doc title="Data table app" desc="DataTable with sorting and filtering — demonstrates trigger_args and DataTable css dict">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Data Table Example using inguitive framework.

Demonstrates:
- Rendering tabular data with the DataTable component
- Using list of dictionaries as the data structure
- Dynamic column ordering via state management
- Dynamic CSS styling with dictionary-based css parameter
- Dynamic data rendering with state management
- Table sorting with toggleable ascending/descending order
- Auto-propagation of state updates (automatic OOB response generation)
- Using get_trigger_args() to access trigger arguments
- Free-text filtering with conditional reset button and status display
- State-based UI controls (show/hide status text and reset button)
- Dynamic layout controls with custom column order and styling

Features:
- Displays a table of employee data
- Sort controls with buttons for each column
- Click a button to sort ascending, click again to sort descending
- Free-text filter across all fields with status display and conditional reset button
- Status text shows &quot;Filter is applied: &lt;text&gt;&quot; when a filter is active
- Reset button appears only when filter is active
- Single interactive table with dynamic column order and custom CSS styling controlled via layout controls
- Table automatically updates when data or layout state changes
- Auto-generated OOB responses from state mutations
- Trigger handlers access arguments via get_trigger_args() without form_data parameter
- Form submission with form_data for dynamic input

Run with: uvicorn examples.data_table_app:app --reload
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import Button, DataTable, Div, Form, Input, State, Text, create_app, get_trigger_args

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)


# --- CSS ---
COLOR_BASE = &quot;slate&quot;
COLOR_100 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-100&quot;
COLOR_200 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-200&quot;
COLOR_300 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-300&quot;
COLOR_400 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-400&quot;
COLOR_700 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-700&quot;
COLOR_900 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-900&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_1 = &quot;blue-700&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2 = &quot;fuchsia-600&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT = &quot;fuchsia-500&quot;
BUTTON_SHAPE = &quot;px-3 py-2 rounded-md font-semibold cursor-pointer shadow-lg active:shadow-none&quot;
BUTTON_PRIMARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_BRAND_1} to-{COLOR_BRAND_2} text-{COLOR_100} hover:to-{COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT} active:to-{COLOR_BRAND_2}&quot;
BUTTON_SECONDARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_400} to-{COLOR_300} text-{COLOR_900} hover:to-{COLOR_200} active:to-{COLOR_300}&quot;


# --- Sample Data ---
# Employee data as list of dictionaries
EMPLOYEE_DATA = [
    {&quot;id&quot;: 1, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Alice Johnson&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;Engineering&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 75000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;Active&quot;},
    {&quot;id&quot;: 2, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Charlie Brown&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;Sales&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 65000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;Active&quot;},
    {&quot;id&quot;: 3, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Bob Smith&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;Engineering&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 80000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;Active&quot;},
    {&quot;id&quot;: 4, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Diana Prince&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;Marketing&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 68000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;On Leave&quot;},
    {&quot;id&quot;: 5, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Frank Castle&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;HR&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 72000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;Active&quot;},
    {&quot;id&quot;: 6, &quot;name&quot;: &quot;Eve Adams&quot;, &quot;department&quot;: &quot;Engineering&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;: 85000, &quot;status&quot;: &quot;Active&quot;},
]

# State to store table data (allows dynamic updates)
employee_data_state = State(EMPLOYEE_DATA, &quot;employee_data_state&quot;)

# State to track sort configuration
sort_config_state = State({&quot;column&quot;: None, &quot;direction&quot;: &quot;asc&quot;}, &quot;sort_config_state&quot;)

# State to track filter text (empty string = no filter)
filter_text_state = State(&quot;&quot;, &quot;filter_text_state&quot;)

# State for custom column order
column_order_state: State[list[str] | None] = State(None, &quot;column_order_state&quot;)

# State for custom styling (None = default, &quot;custom&quot; = custom CSS)
styling_state: State[str | None] = State(None, &quot;styling_state&quot;)


# --- Trigger Handlers ---
@app.trigger_handler
def sort_employees():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Sort employee table by specified column. Toggles direction on repeated clicks.

    Demonstrates auto-propagation and get_trigger_args() for accessing
    trigger arguments without form_data parameter.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    column = get_trigger_args().get(&quot;column&quot;)
    if not column:
        return &quot;&quot;

    # Get current sort config
    current_config = sort_config_state.get()
    current_column = current_config.get(&quot;column&quot;)

    # Determine new direction
    if current_column == column:
        # Same column: toggle direction
        new_direction = &quot;desc&quot; if current_config.get(&quot;direction&quot;) == &quot;asc&quot; else &quot;asc&quot;
    else:
        # Different column: start with ascending
        new_direction = &quot;asc&quot;

    # Update sort config
    sort_config_state.set({&quot;column&quot;: column, &quot;direction&quot;: new_direction})

    # Sort the data (copy to avoid mutating original)
    data = list(employee_data_state.get())
    if column and column != &quot;none&quot;:
        # Handle None values and missing keys safely
        data.sort(key=lambda x: str(x.get(column, &quot;&quot;) or &quot;&quot;), reverse=(new_direction == &quot;desc&quot;))

    # Update data state - auto-propagation will handle OOB response
    employee_data_state.set(data)

    # No explicit return - auto-propagation generates response automatically


@app.trigger_handler
def filter_employees(form_data: dict):
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Filter employees by text search across all fields.

    Searches all string values in each employee dictionary for the
    filter text (case-insensitive). Sets filter_text_state to the search
    text when filter is applied, empty string when cleared.

    Demonstrates:
    - Form data access via form_data parameter
    - Free-text search across multiple fields
    - State management for UI feedback
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    search_text = form_data.get(&quot;filter-text&quot;, &quot;&quot;).lower()

    if search_text:
        # Filter data: search across all fields
        filtered = [e for e in EMPLOYEE_DATA if any(search_text in str(v).lower() for v in e.values())]
        filter_text_state.set(search_text)
    else:
        # Empty search = show all (clear filter)
        filtered = list(EMPLOYEE_DATA)
        filter_text_state.set(&quot;&quot;)

    employee_data_state.set(filtered)

    # Auto-propagation handles OOB response - no explicit return needed


@app.trigger_handler
def clear_filter():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Clear the current filter, showing all employees.

    Resets employee data to full list and clears filter_text_state.
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    filter_text_state.set(&quot;&quot;)
    employee_data_state.set(list(EMPLOYEE_DATA))

    # Auto-propagation handles OOB response - no explicit return needed


@app.trigger_handler
def reset_layout():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Reset table to original layout and default styling.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    column_order_state.set(None)
    styling_state.set(None)


@app.trigger_handler
def set_custom_column_order():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Set custom column order: name, department, salary, status (omitting id).&quot;&quot;&quot;
    column_order_state.set([&quot;department&quot;, &quot;name&quot;, &quot;status&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;])


@app.trigger_handler
def set_custom_styling():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Set custom CSS styling for the table.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    styling_state.set(&quot;custom&quot;)


# --- Components ---
def DynamicEmployeeTable():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Single table that responds to column_order_state and styling_state.

    Demonstrates:
    - Dynamic column ordering via state
    - Dynamic CSS styling via state
    - Single component responding to multiple states
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def dynamic_columns():
        return column_order_state.get()

    def dynamic_css():
        styling = styling_state.get()
        if styling == &quot;custom&quot;:
            css_config = {
                &quot;table&quot;: f&quot;w-full border-2 border-{COLOR_BRAND_1}&quot;,
                &quot;header&quot;: f&quot;px-3 py-2 bg-{COLOR_BRAND_1} text-{COLOR_100} font-mono&quot;,
                &quot;cell&quot;: f&quot;px-3 py-2 border border-{COLOR_700} text-{COLOR_100} font-mono&quot;,
                &quot;row&quot;: f&quot;hover:bg-{COLOR_700} transition-colors&quot;,
            }
        else:
            css_config = &quot;w-full&quot;
        return css_config

    return DataTable(
        data=employee_data_state.get,
        # Note that listen_to also accepts multiple state names.
        listen_to=[&quot;employee_data_state&quot;, &quot;column_order_state&quot;, &quot;styling_state&quot;],
        columns=dynamic_columns,
        css=dynamic_css,
    )


def SortButtons():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render sort buttons for each column in the employee table.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    # Column names and their display labels
    columns = [&quot;id&quot;, &quot;name&quot;, &quot;department&quot;, &quot;salary&quot;, &quot;status&quot;]
    column_labels = [&quot;ID&quot;, &quot;Name&quot;, &quot;Department&quot;, &quot;Salary&quot;, &quot;Status&quot;]

    buttons = []
    for col, label in zip(columns, column_labels):
        buttons.append(
            Button(
                label,
                trigger=&quot;sort_employees&quot;,
                trigger_args={&quot;column&quot;: col},
                css=BUTTON_PRIMARY if col == &quot;id&quot; else BUTTON_SECONDARY,
            )
        )

    return Div(
        Text(
            &quot;Sort tables by:&quot;,
            css=f&quot;font-medium text-{COLOR_300}&quot;,
        ),
        Div(*buttons, css=&quot;flex flex-wrap gap-3&quot;),
        css=&quot;space-y-3&quot;,
    )


def FilterControls():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Filter UI with input field, status text, and conditional reset button.

    Demonstrates:
    - Form component with Input and Button
    - Conditional rendering based on state (status text and reset button)
    - Trigger handlers for form submission
    - Free-text search across all fields
    - State-based conditional UI with text display
    &quot;&quot;&quot;

    def dynamic_text():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return status text based on whether filter is applied.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        filter_text = filter_text_state.get()
        if filter_text:
            return f&quot;Filter is applied: {filter_text}&quot;
        return &quot;&quot;

    def dynamic_css():
        &quot;&quot;&quot;Return CSS for status div based on whether filter is applied.&quot;&quot;&quot;
        if filter_text_state.get():
            return &quot;flex items-center gap-3&quot;
        return &quot;hidden&quot;

    return Div(
        Text(
            &quot;Filter:&quot;,
            css=f&quot;font-medium text-{COLOR_300}&quot;,
        ),
        Form(
            Input(
                id=&quot;filter-text&quot;,
                name=&quot;filter-text&quot;,
                placeholder=&quot;Filter by any keyword...&quot;,
                css=f&quot;px-3 py-2 bg-{COLOR_300} rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Filter&quot;,
                type=&quot;submit&quot;,
                css=BUTTON_PRIMARY,
            ),
            trigger=&quot;filter_employees&quot;,
            css=&quot;flex items-center gap-3&quot;,
        ),
        Div(
            Text(
                dynamic_text,
                css=f&quot;text-{COLOR_300}&quot;,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Reset&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;clear_filter&quot;,
                listen_to=&quot;filter_text_state&quot;,
                css=BUTTON_SECONDARY,
            ),
            listen_to=&quot;filter_text_state&quot;,
            css=dynamic_css,
        ),
        css=&quot;space-y-3&quot;,
    )


def LayoutControls():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Controls for toggling table column order and styling.

    Demonstrates:
    - Multiple buttons controlling different state aspects
    - State-based layout customization
    &quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Text(
            &quot;Customize table layout:&quot;,
            css=f&quot;font-medium text-{COLOR_300}&quot;,
        ),
        Div(
            Button(
                &quot;Original Layout&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;reset_layout&quot;,
                css=BUTTON_PRIMARY,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Custom Column Order&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;set_custom_column_order&quot;,
                css=BUTTON_SECONDARY,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Custom Styling&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;set_custom_styling&quot;,
                css=BUTTON_SECONDARY,
            ),
            css=&quot;flex gap-3&quot;,
        ),
        css=&quot;space-y-3&quot;,
    )


# --- Pages ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def index():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render the main data table example page.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Div(
            Div(
                Text(
                    &quot;Data Table Example&quot;,
                    css=f&quot;text-3xl font-bold text-{COLOR_100}&quot;,
                ),
                Text(
                    &quot;Demonstrates the DataTable component with list of dictionaries data structure.&quot;,
                    css=f&quot;text-lg text-{COLOR_300}&quot;,
                ),
            ),
            Div(
                # Sort controls
                SortButtons(),
                # Filter controls
                FilterControls(),
                # Layout controls
                LayoutControls(),
                css=f&quot;border-2 border-{COLOR_700} rounded-xl p-6 space-y-6&quot;,
            ),
            # Dynamic table demonstrating column order and styling
            DynamicEmployeeTable(),
            css=&quot;w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto p-6 space-y-6&quot;,
        ),
        css=f&quot;w-full bg-{COLOR_900} min-h-screen&quot;,
    )


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.data_table_app:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc><doc title="Registration form" desc="Form handling — demonstrates Form, Input, Textarea, and form data access">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Registration form example using inguitive framework.

Demonstrates form components (Form, Input, Button, Label) with reactive state.

Run with: uvicorn examples.registration_form:app --reload
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import (
    Button,
    Checkbox,
    Div,
    Form,
    Input,
    Label,
    Radio,
    Select,
    State,
    Text,
    Textarea,
    create_app,
    update_components,
)

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)

# --- State Instances ---
# Use collective State for form data (simpler pattern)
form_state = State({}, &quot;form_state&quot;)


# --- Trigger Handlers ---
@app.trigger_handler
async def register(form_data: dict) -&gt; str:
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Handle form submission with auto-injected form_data.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = form_state.get()
    new_data = {**current, **form_data}

    # Handle checkboxes: &quot;on&quot; -&gt; True
    if &quot;terms&quot; in new_data:
        new_data[&quot;terms&quot;] = new_data[&quot;terms&quot;] == &quot;on&quot;

    form_state.set(new_data)
    return update_components(&quot;form_display&quot;)


# --- Helper Functions ---
def GenderRadio(id: str, value: str, label: str) -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    return Div(Radio(id=id, name=&quot;gender&quot;, value=value), Label(label, for_=id), css=&quot;flex items-baseline gap-2&quot;)


def name_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return name text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Name: {form_state.get().get(&#x27;name&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;)}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;name&quot;) else &quot;Name:&quot;


def email_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return email text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Email: {form_state.get().get(&#x27;email&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;)}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;email&quot;) else &quot;Email:&quot;


def password_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return password text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Password: {&#x27;*&#x27; * len(form_state.get().get(&#x27;password&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;))}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;password&quot;) else &quot;Password:&quot;


def bio_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return bio text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Bio: {form_state.get().get(&#x27;bio&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;)}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;bio&quot;) else &quot;Bio:&quot;


def country_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return country text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Country: {form_state.get().get(&#x27;country&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;)}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;country&quot;) else &quot;Country:&quot;


def terms_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return terms text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Terms accepted: {&#x27;Yes&#x27; if form_state.get().get(&#x27;terms&#x27;) else &#x27;No&#x27;}&quot;


def gender_text():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return gender text for display.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return f&quot;Gender: {form_state.get().get(&#x27;gender&#x27;, &#x27;&#x27;)}&quot; if form_state.get().get(&quot;gender&quot;) else &quot;Gender:&quot;


# --- Registration Form Component ---
def RegistrationForm() -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Registration form with reactive state updates.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        # Form
        Form(
            Input(
                id=&quot;name&quot;,
                placeholder=&quot;Enter your name&quot;,
                css=&quot;w-full p-2 border border-zinc-500 rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Input(
                id=&quot;email&quot;,
                type=&quot;email&quot;,
                placeholder=&quot;Enter your email&quot;,
                css=&quot;w-full p-2 border border-zinc-500 rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Input(
                id=&quot;password&quot;,
                type=&quot;password&quot;,
                placeholder=&quot;Enter your password&quot;,
                css=&quot;w-full p-2 border border-zinc-500 rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Textarea(
                id=&quot;bio&quot;,
                placeholder=&quot;Tell us about yourself&quot;,
                rows=3,
                css=&quot;w-full p-2 border border-zinc-500 rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Select(
                id=&quot;country&quot;,
                options=[(&quot;us&quot;, &quot;United States&quot;), (&quot;de&quot;, &quot;Germany&quot;), (&quot;fr&quot;, &quot;France&quot;)],
                css=&quot;w-full p-2 border border-zinc-500 rounded-md&quot;,
            ),
            Div(
                Checkbox(id=&quot;terms&quot;, name=&quot;terms&quot;),
                Label(&quot;I agree to the terms and conditions&quot;, for_=&quot;terms&quot;),
                css=&quot;flex items-baseline gap-2&quot;,
            ),
            Div(
                GenderRadio(id=&quot;gender-male&quot;, value=&quot;male&quot;, label=&quot;Male&quot;),
                GenderRadio(id=&quot;gender-female&quot;, value=&quot;female&quot;, label=&quot;Female&quot;),
                GenderRadio(id=&quot;gender-other&quot;, value=&quot;other&quot;, label=&quot;Other&quot;),
                css=&quot;flex items-baseline gap-6&quot;,
            ),
            Button(
                &quot;Register&quot;,
                type=&quot;submit&quot;,
                trigger=&quot;register&quot;,
                css=&quot;w-full bg-blue-500 text-white font-bold p-2 rounded-md hover:bg-blue-600&quot;,
            ),
            css=&quot;space-y-6 max-w-md mx-auto p-6 bg-white rounded-xl shadow-md&quot;,
        ),
        # Confirmation display
        Div(
            Text(
                name_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                email_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                password_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                bio_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            Text(
                country_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            Text(terms_text, css=&quot;text-center&quot;),
            Text(
                gender_text,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            id=&quot;form_display&quot;,
            css=&quot;text-xl font-bold mt-6 space-y-3&quot;,
        ),
        css=&quot;w-full min-h-screen flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-6&quot;,
    )


# --- Routes ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def home():
    return RegistrationForm()


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.registration_form:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc><doc title="Navigation demo" desc="Link vs trigger patterns — demonstrates when to use Link(href=) vs trigger=">&quot;&quot;&quot;
Navigation Demo example using inguitive framework.

Demonstrates two approaches to navigation:
1. Traditional multi-page navigation (URL changes)
2. SPA-style content switching (URL stays constant, content changes)

Run with: uvicorn examples.navigation_demo:app --reload
&quot;&quot;&quot;

from pathlib import Path

from inguitive import Button, Div, Link, State, Text, create_app, redirect, update_components

# --- App Setup ---
app = create_app(template_dir=Path(__file__).parent.parent / &quot;templates&quot;)


# --- CSS ---
COLOR_BASE = &quot;slate&quot;
COLOR_100 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-100&quot;
COLOR_200 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-200&quot;
COLOR_300 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-300&quot;
COLOR_400 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-400&quot;
COLOR_900 = f&quot;{COLOR_BASE}-900&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_1 = &quot;blue-700&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2 = &quot;fuchsia-600&quot;
COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT = &quot;fuchsia-500&quot;
BUTTON_SHAPE = &quot;p-3 rounded-md font-semibold cursor-pointer shadow-lg active:shadow-none&quot;
BUTTON_PRIMARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_BRAND_1} to-{COLOR_BRAND_2} text-{COLOR_100} hover:to-{COLOR_BRAND_2_LIGHT}&quot;
BUTTON_SECONDARY = f&quot;{BUTTON_SHAPE} bg-linear-to-tr from-{COLOR_400} to-{COLOR_300} text-{COLOR_900} hover:to-{COLOR_200}&quot;
PAGE = f&quot;min-h-screen bg-{COLOR_900} flex justify-center items-center&quot;
CARD = &quot;max-w-2xl mx-auto p-6 space-y-6 bg-white rounded-xl shadow-md&quot;
TEXT_CONTAINER = &quot;w-full flex flex-grow p-6 rounded-lg bg-white justify-center items-center&quot;


# --- State Instances ---
# State to track which content variant is shown on page 1
content_state = State(&quot;a&quot;, &quot;content_state&quot;)


# --- Trigger Handler ---
@app.trigger_handler
def switch_content():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Toggle between content variants a and b.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    current = content_state.get()
    new = &quot;b&quot; if current == &quot;a&quot; else &quot;a&quot;
    content_state.set(new)
    return update_components(*content_state.listeners)


# --- Component Functions ---
def PageHeader(title: str, color: str = &quot;black&quot;) -&gt; Text:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return a page header with the given title.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Text(title, css=f&quot;text-3xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-{color}&quot;)


def Divider(color: str) -&gt; Div:  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return a horizontal divider.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Div(&quot;&quot;, css=f&quot;border-t border-{color}/20 flex-1&quot;),
        Text(&quot;OR&quot;, css=f&quot;text-center font-semibold text-{color}&quot;),
        Div(&quot;&quot;, css=f&quot;border-t border-{color}/20 flex-1&quot;),
        css=&quot;w-full flex flex-row items-center gap-3&quot;,
    )


def SwitchPageButton(href: str, label: str):  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return a button that navigates to a different page.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Link(
        Button(
            label,
            css=f&quot;{BUTTON_PRIMARY} w-full&quot;,
        ),
        href=href,
        css=&quot;block&quot;,
    )


def SwitchContentButton():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return a button that switches content on the same page.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Button(
        &quot;Switch Content&quot;,
        trigger=&quot;switch_content&quot;,
        css=f&quot;{BUTTON_SECONDARY} w-full&quot;,
    )


def ContentA():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return content variant A.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        PageHeader(&quot;Page 1 - Content A&quot;),
        # Hint for traditional navigation
        Text(
            &quot;Click &#x27;Go to Page 2&#x27; below to navigate to Page 2. &quot;
            &quot;Watch how the URL in your browser&#x27;s address bar changes.&quot;
        ),
        # Link for traditional navigation (URL changes)
        SwitchPageButton(href=&quot;/page2&quot;, label=&quot;Go to Page 2&quot;),
        Divider(color=&quot;black&quot;),
        # Hint for SPA content switching
        Text(
            &quot;Click &#x27;Switch Content&#x27; below to change the content on this page. &quot;
            &quot;Notice how the content changes but the URL stays the same.&quot;
        ),
        # Button for SPA content switching (URL stays)
        SwitchContentButton(),
        css=CARD,
    )


def ContentB():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return content variant B.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        PageHeader(&quot;Page 1 - Content B&quot;, color=&quot;white&quot;),
        Div(SwitchPageButton(href=&quot;/page2&quot;, label=&quot;Go to Page 2&quot;), SwitchContentButton(), css=&quot;grid grid-cols-2 gap-6&quot;),
        Div(
            Text(
                &quot;Click &#x27;Go to Page 2&#x27; to navigate to Page 2. Watch how the URL in your browser&#x27;s address bar changes.&quot;,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            css=TEXT_CONTAINER,
        ),
        Divider(color=&quot;white&quot;),
        Div(
            Text(
                &quot;Click &#x27;Switch Content&#x27; to change the content on this page. &quot;
                &quot;Notice how the content changes but the URL stays the same.&quot;,
                css=&quot;text-center&quot;,
            ),
            css=TEXT_CONTAINER,
        ),
        css=&quot;max-w-6xl min-h-screen mx-auto p-6 space-y-6 flex flex-col&quot;,
    )


# --- Page Component Functions ---
# --- Helper Functions ---
def dynamic_content():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Return content A or B based on content_state.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return ContentA() if content_state.get() == &quot;a&quot; else ContentB()

def Page1():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Page 1 demonstrating traditional navigation and SPA content switching.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        dynamic_content,
        css=PAGE,
        listen_to=&quot;content_state&quot;,
    )


def Page2():  # noqa: N802
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Page 2 demonstrating traditional navigation and SPA content switching.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Div(
        Div(
            PageHeader(&quot;Page 2&quot;),
            # Hint for traditional navigation
            Text(
                &quot;Click &#x27;Go to Page 1&#x27; below to navigate to Page 1. &quot;
                &quot;Watch how the URL in your browser&#x27;s address bar changes.&quot;
            ),
            # Link for traditional navigation (URL changes)
            SwitchPageButton(href=&quot;/page1&quot;, label=&quot;Go to Page 1&quot;),
            css=CARD,
        ),
        css=PAGE,
    )


# --- Routes ---
@app.page(&quot;/&quot;)
def index():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Redirect to Page 1.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return redirect(&quot;/page1&quot;)


@app.page(&quot;/page1&quot;)
def page1():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render Page 1.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Page1()


@app.page(&quot;/page2&quot;)
def page2():
    &quot;&quot;&quot;Render Page 2.&quot;&quot;&quot;
    return Page2()


# --- Start ---
if __name__ == &quot;__main__&quot;:
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(&quot;examples.navigation_demo:app&quot;, host=&quot;0.0.0.0&quot;, port=8000, reload=True)
</doc></docs></project>
